note:
在大多数社会中，很容易量化男女之间的差异。例如，女性往往寿命更长，但收入却更低。从历史上看，一直存在将这些差异归因于生物学的强烈趋势。但是大多数社会对待妇女的方式截然不同，这使得难以区分的生物学和社会因素成为一个挑战。本周，几篇论文运用了一些有趣的方法来使两者分开。
 在一项研究中，研究人员考察了中国的母系社会，以探讨性别规范对健康的影响。第二，详细调查探讨了内在期望如何影响美国的工程职业成功。
  关于中国的工作集中于妇女的健康。由于女性比男性长寿，您可能会期望她们总体上健康。你会错的；女人的疾病负担往往比男人高。为了暗示为什么可能会发生这种情况，研究人员研究了一个叫做摩梭的族群，该族群居住在云南和四川两省交界的西藏附近地区。摩梭社会的一些成员采用父权制，男性为户主。但是其他人则以妇女为户主，而丈夫则继续与他们长大的家庭生活在一起。孩子由母亲抚养长大，仍然是家庭的一部分。
 这种情况使研究人员可以进行简单的实验，检查母系和父系家庭中是否患有慢性疾病。他们专注于炎症和高血压的症状，这是两种对健康有重大影响的疾病，已知受社会因素影响。
    在实行父权制的社区，慢性炎症的发生率是女性的两倍（8.3％比3.2％）。 33％的女性患有高血压，而男性为26％。在母系社区进行测试极大地改变了这种情况。那里，慢性炎症袭击了男性的6.4％，但女性的只有3.6％。高血压也有类似的结果，其中男性占28％，女性仅占26％。研究人员指出，男性的统计数据基本上没有变化，这归因于他们从长大后的家庭获得的高水平社会支持。
总体而言，很难得出结论，正如作者所说，“健康在很大程度上受到社会和文化因素的影响，包括影响自治，资源控制和社会支持的性别规范”。成为一家之主可能会带来一些压力，但掌控家庭资源并不能完全抵消这些压力。
  第二组研究人员决定研究美国的工程师。在为期三年的时间里，他们跟踪了来自27所不同大学的工程和计算机科学课程的人员，获得了559名学生的数据。除其他外，调查参与者报告了他们的等级，起薪以及他们最终从事的领域。但是，他们还进行了一项简短调查，以评估作者所说的“工程自我效能”，并询问他们他们对自己执行诸如构建原型产品，构建数学模型等任务的能力充满信心。
 与这些领域的典型情况一样，研究人员发现，从工程专业毕业的女性平均收入较低，每年的差额接近5,000美元。在考虑了GPA和大学等因素之后，差距略有缩小，但仍然持续存在。
    但是一旦人们对自己技能的信心被用来调整结果，工资差距就会缩小到微不足道的程度。换句话说，似乎最能解释性别工资差距的因素是毕业生对工程师能力的信心。换句话说，女工程师相对于学校和GPA可能会低估其可能的技能，而较低的信心似乎使他们付出了代价。
 但是可以肯定的是，研究人员还研究了针对女性低薪的另外两种解释。一个建议是，金钱对女性没有多大影响，但是在这个样本中，对工资的重视程度并没有性别差异。已经提出的另一种解释是，妇女优先考虑健康的工作场所文化而不是薪水。事实证明这是事实，但对于男人和女人而言，重视工作场所文化与更高的薪水相关。
自信是分隔男人和女人的主要因素，这一事实不足为奇。正如研究人员指出的那样，“先前的工作表明，女孩和妇女对数学和科学能力的信心低于男孩和男人，这是这些学科实际能力的净值。男孩已经表明自己愿意说他们已经掌握了虚假的数学概念，这是这种过度自信的结果。但是，如果我们想让更多的女性从事STEM事业并留在那里，那么我们必须考虑如何解决放错信心的性别差距。
 更笼统地说，这两个报告将社会因素在性别差异中发挥的巨大作用带回家了，这通常归因于先天的生物学因素。这并不意味着我们知道如何补偿这些因素，但这是迈向实现存在可以修复的关键的第一步。
note_en:
In most societies, there are very easy to quantify differences between men and women. Women tend to live longer but earn less, for example. Historically, there has been a strong tendency to ascribe those differences to biology. But most societies treat women very differently, making disentangling biological and societal factors a challenge. This week, a couple of papers apply some interesting approaches to teasing the two apart.
 In one, researchers looked at a matrilineal society in China to explore gender norms' impact on health. In the second, a detailed survey explored how internalized expectations can influence engineering career success in the US.
  The work on China focused on women's health. Since women outlive men, you might expect that they're generally healthy. You'd be wrong; women tend to have a higher disease burden than men do. To get a hint as to why that might be the case, the researchers looked at an ethnic group called the Mosuo, who occupy an area near Tibet, on the border between Yunnan and Sichuan provinces. Some members of the Mosuo society have adopted patriarchal practices, with males as the head of the household. But others have women as the head of household, while their husbands continue to live with the families they grew up in. Children are raised by their mothers and remain part of her household.
 This situation allowed the researchers to perform a simple experiment, checking for signs of chronic disease in the matrilineal and patrilineal households. They focused on signs of inflammation and hypertension, two disorders with significant health impacts that are known to be influenced by social factors.
    In communities that followed patrilineal practices, chronic inflammation was over twice as prevalent in women (8.3 percent vs. 3.2 percent). Thirty-three percent of women were hypertensive, compared to 26 percent of men. Testing in a matrilineal community changed the situation dramatically. There, chronic inflammation hit 6.4 percent of men but only 3.6 percent of women. Similar outcomes were seen with hypertension, which struck 28 percent of men but only 26 percent of women. The researchers note that the stats for men are largely unchanged, which they ascribe to the high levels of social support they get from living in the household they grew up in.
 Overall, it's hard to escape the conclusion that, as the authors put it, "health is shaped extensively by social and cultural factors, including gender norms that impact autonomy, resource control, and social support." Being head of household might come with some stresses, but they're more than offset by having control over the household resources.
  A second group of researchers decided to look at engineers in the US. For a period of three years, they tracked people coming out of engineering and computer science programs at 27 different universities, obtaining data on 559 students. Among other things, the survey participants reported their grades, starting salaries, and what field they ended up working in. But they also took a short survey that gauged what the authors term their "engineering self-efficacy," in which they were asked about their confidence in their ability to perform tasks like constructing prototype products, building mathematical models, and so on.
 As is typical in these fields, the researchers found that the women who graduated from engineering programs received lower pay on average, with the gap being nearly $5,000 a year. And after taking factors like GPA and university into account, the gap shrank slightly but still persisted.
    But once people's confidence in their own skills was used to adjust the outcome, the wage gap shrank to insignificance. In other words, the factor that seemed to account for the gender-specific wage gap best was the graduates' confidence in their abilities as engineers. Put differently, female engineers are more likely to underrate their likely skills relative to what their school and GPA might suggest, and that lower confidence seems to cost them when it comes to pay.
 Just to be sure, however, the researchers looked at two other explanations that have been offered for women's lower pay. One is the suggestion that money doesn't matter as much to women, but in this sample, there was no gender difference in the importance attached to salary. Another explanation that has been suggested is that women prioritize a healthier workplace culture over salary. This turned out to be true, but for both men and women, placing a high value on workplace culture was associated with higher salaries.
 The fact that confidence is a major factor that separates men and women shouldn't be surprising. As the researchers note, "Prior work indicates that girls and women have lower confidence in their math and science ability than boys and men, net of actual ability in these subjects." And boys have shown themselves willing to  say they've mastered fake math concepts as an outgrowth of that overconfidence. But if we want more women going into STEM careers and staying there, then we have to think about how to account for the gender gap in misplaced confidence.
 More generally, the two reports drive home the huge effects that social factors can play in gender difference that have often been ascribed to innate, biological factors. That doesn't mean we know how to compensate for these factors, but it's a key first step toward realizing that there's something there that can be fixed.
title: 内部化的以性别为中心的态度影响健康，职业前景
title_en: Internalized gender-focused attitudes affect health, career prospects

note:
今天，美国国家科学基金会宣布将关闭其著名的阿雷西博无线电天文台。天文台的主菜建在波多黎各的山顶上，跨度超过300米，而且其巨大的尺寸使其成为从詹姆斯·邦德电影到电子游戏等流行文化的特色。但是，尽管有很长的科学贡献历史，但​​该观测台在十年前一直在努力筹集资金，而支持它的两条电缆今年都失败了，使其处于不稳定的状态。
 工程研究确定无法在不使工人面临风险的情况下修理硬件，NSF决定关闭天文台。
  尽管Arecibo主菜的绝对规模吸引了最多的注意力，但该菜纯粹是反射器。望远镜的实际业务端是感知无线电波的地方，它是一个仪器平台，上面悬挂着三座塔架上的电缆，悬挂在其上方。仪器平台装有一个接收器，可以将其移动到磁盘上方的不同位置，从而使其能够解析比固定碟所提示的更多方向的信号。
 自从1960年代启用以来，该天文台在许多发现中发挥了作用，主要是在脉冲星领域，脉冲星是一类辐射中子星。它也参与了SETI搜索，并在假设任何智能生命都可能参与其自己的SETI程序的前提下，将图像传输到星团。但是在过去的15年左右的时间里，阿雷西博（Arecibo）的主要支持手段NSF削减了对天文台的资金，该天文台在此期间一直难以维持全面运作。
 最终，不是钱注定了阿雷西博。相反，它是仪器平台。今年八月，帮助支撑平台的辅助电缆之一折断，在下面的无线电反射盘中产生了缝隙。在制定更换电缆和修理天线的计划时（更换电缆已经在订购中），同一塔上的7.5厘米主电缆之一已于11月6日抢购。
工程分析随后确定，尽管电缆上的应变仅为其最小断裂强度的60％左右，但仍发生了电缆故障。这就提出了有关剩余电缆的稳定性以及结构支撑其仪器平台的能力的严重问题。分析得出结论，发现是不安全的。平台可能会在没有警告的情况下倒塌，并且任何断裂的电缆都会对塔上的所有工人造成危险，因为大型电缆在断裂后会以极高的速度移动。在美国国家科学基金会（NSF）和中佛罗里达大学（University of Central Florida）咨询的另外三家工程公司中，有两家同意这项评估。
 NSF的拉尔夫·高美（Ralph Gaume）说：“在进行这些评估之前，我们的问题不是是否应该修复天文台，而是如何修复。” “但是最后，大量数据表明，我们无法安全地做到这一点。这是我们无法跨越的界线。”
  目前，NSF只是宣布它将开始计划退役过程。将数据存档从仪器移至云服务的工作已经在进行中，安全删除平台的计划显然将涉及许多其他工程分析。美国国家科学基金会（NSF）表示，与该站点相关的其他一些科学计划将保持开放状态。该天文台还是科学界与波多黎各社会之间联系的主要重点，要了解如何在缺乏有效仪器的情况下保持这种动态还需要进一步研究。
 很难与风险分析争论。不难想象，更现代，更灵活的仪器能够以较低的成本完成我们期望从Arecibo获得的科学，这解释了Arecibo为何在十年前一直在努力筹集资金。考虑到这两个因素，很明显Arecibo的时代已经到来。
note_en:
Today, the National Science Foundation announced that its famed Arecibo radio observatory would be shut down. Built into a hilltop in Puerto Rico, the main dish of the observatory is over 300 meters across, and its massive size has made it a  feature in popular culture ranging from James Bond movies to video games. But despite a long history of scientific contributions, the observatory has been struggling for funding for over a decade, and two cables that support it have failed this year, leaving it in a precarious state.
 After engineering studies determined there was no way to repair the hardware without putting workers at risk, the NSF made the decision to shut the observatory down.
  While the sheer scale of the main dish at Arecibo grabbed the most attention, the dish was purely a reflector. The actual business end of the telescope, where radio waves were sensed, was an instrument platform suspended high above it by cables strung from three towers. The instrument platform held a receiver that could be moved to different locations above the disk, giving it the ability to resolve signals from more directions than its fixed dish might suggest.
 Since its commissioning in the 1960s, the observatory has played a role in many discoveries, primarily in the field of pulsars, a class of radio-emitting neutron stars. It has also been involved with SETI searches, and it transmitted an image to a star cluster under the assumption that any intelligent life there might be partaking in its own SETI program. But over the last 15 years or so the NSF, Arecibo's primary means of support, has cut its funding for the observatory, which has struggled to maintain full operations over this period.
 But it wasn't money that eventually doomed Arecibo; instead, it was the instrument platform. In August of this year, one of the auxiliary cables that help support the platform snapped, creating a gash in the radio-reflective dish below. While plans were being made to replace that cable and repair the dish—replacement cables were already on order—one of the 7.5cm main cables on the same tower snapped on November 6.
    An engineering analysis subsequently determined that this cable failure happened despite the fact that the strain on it was only about 60 percent of what should be its minimum breaking strength. This raised serious questions about the stability of the remaining cables, and thus the ability of the structure to support its instrument platform. The analysis concluded that it was unsafe to find out; the platform could collapse without warning, and any snapped cables would present a danger to any workers on the towers, as the large cables would move at very high speeds following a break. Of the three additional engineering firms consulted by NSF and the University of Central Florida, two agreed with this assessment.
 "Until these assessments came in, our question was not if the observatory should be repaired but how," said the NSF's Ralph Gaume. "But in the end, a preponderance of data showed that we simply could not do this safely. And that is a line we cannot cross."
  Right now, the NSF is simply announcing that it will start planning for the decommissioning process. Work was already underway to move the data archives from the instrument onto a cloud service, and the plans for safely removing the platform will obviously involve a lot of additional engineering analyses. The NSF said that several other scientific programs associated with the site will remain open. The observatory also serves as a major focus of outreach between the scientific community and Puerto Rican society—understanding how to maintain that dynamic in the absence of an active instrument will also require further study.
 It's difficult to argue with the risk analysis. And it's not difficult to imagine that more modern and flexible instruments can accomplish the science we'd expect from Arecibo at a lower cost, which explains why Arecibo has been struggling for funding for over a decade. Given both of these factors, it's clear that Arecibo's time has come.
title: 阿雷西博天文台的大菜已经到了终点
title_en: Big dish of Arecibo observatory has reached the end of the line

note:
AT＆T宣布对DirecTV卫星和U-verse TV服务进行另一轮价格上涨，从2021年1月17日开始，每月价格将上涨至9美元。
 AT＆T在其网站上的通知中说：“由于编程成本增加，我们正在调整视频套餐的价格。” “定期，电视网络所有者会增加向DirecTV收取其播放电影，节目和体育赛事的权利的费用。”当然，由于AT＆T拥有时代华纳（Time Warner），因此它本身就决定了其中一些编程价格。
 DirecTV的160频道“娱乐”套餐每月将增加5美元，该套餐目前的标准费率为每月97美元。 185个频道的Choice套餐每月增加7美元，目前为每月115美元。 250声道的Ultimate套装（当前为142美元）和330声道的Premier套装（当前为197美元）将增加9美元。
 新客户可以在12个月后到期的促销价下获得这些套餐，价格在64.99美元至134.99美元之间。 AT＆T说：“如果您目前有DirecTV电视促销活动，您将一直保留该折扣，直到到期为止。” “促销期结束后，您将为包裹支付新的价格。”
 DirecTV为国际客户提供的Basic和Preferred Choice软件包也分别增加了$ 1和$ 3，某些西班牙语软件包增加了$ 6，而“ Xtra”软件包增加了$ 8。只有最低服务，家庭和ChineseDirect Plus计划没有增加。
AT＆T将U-verse电视的价格提高了5美元至9美元（视包装而定），同时保持最基本的U-verse电视包装的价格不变。 U-verse通过AT＆T的有线网络提供电视。与DirecTV一样，采用U-verse促销价格的客户在促销期结束之前不会看到价格上涨。
  DirecTV还增加了“每月0.19美元的联邦成本回收费”，类似于过去每年收取一次的费用。尽管名称如此，但该费用并非政府强制规定的。 AT＆T说，这笔费用包括“ DirecTV付给联邦通信委员会的费用”。
    虽然这笔费用起步很小，但AT＆T一直在通过增加新费用然后逐步稳定提高价格来偷偷提高整体价格的历史。除了要收取的AT＆T列出的价格中不包括的其他其他费用外，还将收取联邦费用追回费，以便其宣传的价格低于其实际收费。
 从积极的一面来看，AT＆T的公告并未包括地区体育网络（RSN）和广播电视费用的任何增加，而这两项费用都是在一年前提高的。当与Ars今天联系时，AT＆T确认“ RSN费用和广播费用保持不变”。
 信不信由你，AT＆T表示正在降低某些高级频道的价格。其中包括Starz，Cinemax和Showtime的价格降低$ 3。对于某些附加捆绑包，包括体育频道，也最多可以减少$ 3。但是，即使有了付费频道，价格也会有所上涨，包括将附加节目包价格提高2.96美元，其中包括HBO Max，Starz，Showtime，Cinemax和体育频道包。同样，可以在此链接上找到价格变动的完整列表。
电视费用可能会因基本套餐，附加频道和促销价格而异。 AT＆T对DirecTV及其其他高级电视服务的平均每月收费为每位客户130.55美元。
 由于价格上涨，促销优惠的使用减少以及廉价流媒体服务的竞争，AT＆T的客户群急剧下降。 AT＆T目前拥有1,710万高级电视用户，而2017年初为2500万。该类别包括DirecTV卫星，U-verse和更新的AT＆T TV在线服务，后者模仿了带有隐藏费用，合同和第二大有线电视的有线电视，年价格上涨。
 AT＆T一直在寻找DirecTV的买家，据报道，收购要约对这家卫星子公司的估值不到160亿美元-约为AT＆T 2015年为该公司支付的490亿美元的三分之一。接管DirecTV和U-verse的付费电视业务。正如CNBC本月报道的那样，AT＆T正在与私募股权公司进行谈判，“通过一项复杂的交易出售其付费电视业务中的大量少数股权……这将使遗留资产脱离无线运营商的资产负债表。”
 CNBC援引未具名消息人士的话说：“根据拟议中的交易条款，AT＆T将保留业务的多数经济所有权，并将保留包括工厂和光纤在内的U-verse基础设施的所有权。” “买方将控制付费电视的发行业务，并在其帐簿上巩固该业务。”
title: 由于客户流失和可能的销售，AT＆T再次提高了DirecTV的价格
title_en: AT&T raises DirecTV prices again amid customer losses and possible sale

note:
多年来，我把最好的那半场比赛拖到了很多比赛中，当她问我们几个星期前是否可以参观棒球名人堂时，这是一件容易的事。她和我一样热衷于棒球运动，毕竟，她心爱的华盛顿国民队以赢得世界大赛而闻名。从我撰写本文的时间来看，纽约Sure Cooperstown确实确实位于东北偏北6.5小时左右。但是我非常喜欢开车，往返13小时是比大多数了解汽车更好的方式。
 但是哪辆车？幸运的是，我的日历在那一周被预订了两次。红色的角落是2021年的保时捷718 Boxster GTS，带有可再生的4.0升自然吸气发动机，手动变速箱和固定碳纤维桶形座椅。在珍珠白的转角处，有2021年丰田Venza，具有冷却和加热的座椅，自适应巡航控制系统以及混合动力总成，能够实现39mpg（6l / 100km）的总和。您将能够了解到保时捷在不久的将来会多么令人上瘾，但是显然，这对于这项特定工作而言是错误的工具。
 因此，在与丰田公司的好人交涉后，确保我对他们的新跨界车增加了750英里（1,207公里）的同意后，他们确定了当天的休假时间，并填写了所有表格以满足纽约公众的需求健康的人，我们都定了。
  这是第二辆戴上Venza品牌徽章的丰田跨界车，但是如果您告诉我这实际上是雷克萨斯，我会很高兴地相信您。从外观上看，它看起来像是丰田豪华车支的褶皱和有角度的跨界，但脸部柔软，而不是装饰这些车辆的硕大的奶酪刨丝赛隆格栅。但是Venza是它自己的东西，而不是经过重新包装的雷克萨斯（Lexus），并且凭借其105.9英寸（2,690毫米）的轴距，它牢固地位于中型支架中。 （它建立在与RAV4以及凯美瑞和雷克萨斯ES轿车共享的TNGA-K平台上。）
 Venza仅提供EPA额定的219hp（163kW）混合动力总成，在城市中为40mpg（5.9l / 100km），在高速公路上为37mpg（6.4l / 100km）。它结合了2.5升四缸汽油发动机和三个电动机，一个集成的起动机/发电机，一个与发动机配合工作以为前轮提供动力的118 hp（88 kW）驱动电机，以及一个用于发动机前轮的附加54 hp（40 kW）驱动电机。后轮可按需提供全轮驱动。这些电动机从0.9kWh的锂离子电池（并为其充电）而不是1.6kWh的NiMH电池组（并为其充电）获取动力，而我们在其他丰田（非插电式）混合动力汽车中已经见过。
一旦您打开Venza的车门，豪华车的印象就会继续，特别是因为我们的测试车是39,800美元的Venza Limited，其中大多数选项都已打勾。 （范围还包括$ 32,470的Venza LE和$ 36,000的Venza XLE。）
 座椅实际上是用称为SofTex的人造材料包裹的，而不是缝制的皮革，但看起来很聪明，座椅足够舒适，可以在其中坐满半天以上。关于信息娱乐系统，我什至有好几句话要说，该系统在其12.3英寸触摸屏上打开CarPlay窗口，并显示有关汽车状态的相关信息。屏幕明亮清晰，响应灵敏，用户界面并没有像其他丰田汽车那样让我感到烦恼。
 如您所料，我们从哥伦比亚特区到库珀斯敦的路线主要是在高速公路上进行的，Venza不仅可以完成任务。行驶平稳，巡航速度下风声不大，当您偶尔要看一下秋天的落叶时，自适应巡航控制会很有用，如果您考虑周全，则可以超越官方的公路效率数字美好时光。
 到名人堂的最后18英里（29公里）比两条车道的公路更有趣，它们为Venza的Sport模式提供了很好的测试，这使动力总成更具响应性，但其他方面保持不变许多。 （确实，这最后的18英里行驶在红色保时捷上会更加有趣，尽管它肯定不足以弥补到达那里所需的六个小时）。
 社交名人堂访​​问已经完成-最喜欢的Nats制品：Gerardo Parra的小鲨鱼-是时候该爬回船上了，并反向进行此过程。想到再坐6.5个小时，便无可畏惧，这充分说明了Venza的能力。做得好，丰田。
note_en:
After dragging my better half to  a lot of car races over the years, it was an easy yes when she asked if we might visit the Baseball Hall of Fame a few weeks ago. She is as big a fan of baseball as I am of motor racing, and her beloved Washington Nationals were being celebrated for having won the World Series, after all. Sure Cooperstown, New York, does indeed lie nearly 6.5 hours north-northeast from where I write this. But I quite like driving, and a 13-hour round trip is a better way than most to get to know a car.
 But which car? As luck would have it, my calendar was double-booked that week. In the red corner, a 2021 Porsche 718 Boxster GTS with a revvy 4.0L naturally aspirated engine, manual transmission, and fixed carbon fiber bucket seats. In the pearl-white corner, a 2021 Toyota Venza with cooled and heated seats, adaptive cruise control, and a hybrid powertrain capable of a combined 39mpg (6l/100km). You'll be able to read about how addictive that Porsche was in the near future, but obviously it was the wrong tool for this particular job.
 So after checking with the nice people at Toyota to make sure they were OK with me adding 750 miles (1,207km) to their new crossover—they were—the day booked off work, and with all the forms filled in to satisfy New York's public health people, we were all set.
  This is the second Toyota crossover to wear the Venza name badge, but if you told me this one was actually a Lexus I'd happily believe you. Visually, it looks of a piece with the creased and angular crossovers from Toyota's luxury offshoot but with a softer face rather than that giant cheese-grater Cylon grille that adorns those vehicles. But the Venza is its own thing, not a rebadged Lexus, and with its 105.9-inch (2,690mm) wheelbase, it lives firmly in the midsize bracket. (It's built on the TNGA-K platform, which it shares with the RAV4, as well as the  Camry and  Lexus ES sedans.)
 The Venza is offered with just a single 219hp (163kW) hybrid powertrain rated by the EPA at 40mpg (5.9l/100km) in the city and 37mpg (6.4l/100km) on the highway. This combines a 2.5L four-cylinder gasoline engine and three electric motors—an integrated starter/generator, an 118hp (88kW) drive motor that works with the engine to power the front wheels, and an additional 54hp (40kW) drive motor for the rear wheels that provides all-wheel drive on-demand. These electric motors draw power from (and recharge) a 0.9kWh lithium-ion battery instead of the 1.6kWh NiMH pack we're more used to seeing in other Toyota (not-plugin) hybrids.
   The luxury car impression continues once you open the Venza's door, particularly since our test car was the $39,800 Venza Limited with most of the options ticked. (The range also includes the $32,470 Venza LE and the $36,000 Venza XLE.)
 The seats are actually wrapped in an artificial material called SofTex, not stitched leather, but it looks smart, and the seats are comfortable enough to spend more than half a day sitting in them. I even have pleasant things to say about the infotainment system, which windows CarPlay on its 12.3-inch touchscreen next to pertinent information about the car's status. The screen is bright and clear, it's responsive, and the UI didn't aggravate me the way it has in other Toyotas.
 As you might expect, our route from the District of Columbia to Cooperstown was conducted mostly upon the highways, and the Venza was more than up to the task. The ride is smooth, there's not much wind noise at cruising speeds, adaptive cruise control is helpful when you want to take the occasional look at the fall foliage, and if you're thoughtful, you can exceed the official highway efficiency figure while still making good time.
 The final 18 miles (29km) to the Hall of Fame get a little more interesting than a two-lane highway, and they offered a good test for the Venza's Sport mode, which makes the powertrain a little more responsive but otherwise doesn't change much. (It is true that these final 18 miles would have been a good deal more interesting in the red Porsche, although certainly not enough to make up for the six hours in those bucket seats that it would take to get there.)
 Socially distanced Hall of Fame visit complete—favorite Nats artifact: Gerardo Parra's Baby Shark—it was time to climb back aboard and repeat the process in reverse. There was no dread accompanying the thought of another 6.5 hours sat behind the Venza's wheel, and that speaks volumes about how competent it is. Nice work, Toyota.
title: 主流价格的豪华跨界车？ 2021年丰田Venza
title_en: A luxury crossover with a mainstream price? The 2021 Toyota Venza

遥远星球上的一名年轻男女被称为“新世界”，他们逃离了混沌之徒的一个宗教狂热者小镇，这是一部由道格·利曼（Doug Liman）执导的电影，改编自屡获殊荣的科幻三部曲。由Patrick Ness。这部电影还拥有两位巨星：最近在《星球大战》三部曲中饰演Rey的Daisy Ridley和MCEU中蜘蛛侠的最新化身Tom Tom Holland。
 该系列包括三本小说：《放手刀》，《问与答》和《人中的怪物》，以及在同一个虚构的宇宙中布置的三个短篇小说，旨在作为小说的伴侣。系列活动发生在一个名为“新世界”的星球上，在这里我们遇到了托德。托德是一个生活在一个虔诚的定居点的男孩，名叫普伦蒂斯敦（Prentisstown）。没有女人了，所有男人都被一种叫做“噪音”的东西所困扰，这使他们的思想可以被听到，使许多人发疯。托德的小狗曼奇也是如此。据称，这是一种称为Spackle的本土聪明物种的生物战的结果，他对殖民者的到来感到不满。细菌杀死了所有妇女，并给男人留下了噪音。
 有一天，托德在附近的沼泽中发现一片寂静（“噪音中的一个洞”），当他告诉收养父母这件事时，他们坚称他必须逃离普伦蒂斯敦。回到沼泽，托德与小女孩维奥拉面对面，小女孩乘坐小型侦察机坠毁在新世界上，然后是一艘新的行星定居者飞船。市长，他的儿子戴维（Davy）和一个名叫亚伦（Aaron）的邪恶传教士一直在不懈地追求，他们寻求避难于其他更和平的定居点，包括有传言说可以治愈噪音的叫做避风港的小镇，希望找到一个警告即将到来的船只他们可能面临的暴力的方法。
  这部三部曲的标题摘自第一本书的重点：“噪声是一个没有过滤的人，没有过滤器，一个人只是在乱走。”尼斯（Ness）说，他想出三部曲的想法是在思考我们当前的信息超载及其对年轻人的影响时，以及他对在小说中说话的狗“从来没有像...那样说话感到沮丧”。 。”从主题上讲，他将第一本书描述为关于逃亡的书，第二本书描述为暴政，第三本书描述的是战争。
    查理·考夫曼（Charlie Kaufman）撰写了脚本的初稿，但不久后离开了该项目。随后进行了许多脚本修订，Ness亲自为此做出了贡献。 （他还是《怪兽召唤》（A Monster Calls）的作者，并为那部电影改编了剧本。）然后，利曼（Liman）签约导演，雷德利（Ridley）和荷兰（Holland）担任联合主演。影片在蒙特利尔，苏格兰和冰岛拍摄。
从预告片来看，利曼的电影看起来很接近第一本书，尽管托德和维奥拉显然在电影中年龄更大（托德在第一部小说中即将年满13岁）。根据官方前提：
 在不久的将来，托德·休伊特（Tom Hewitt）（汤姆·荷兰）发现了维奥拉（Daisy Ridley），一个坠毁在她星球上的神秘女孩，那里所有的女人都消失了，男人也被“噪音”所困扰。展示他们的所有想法。在这种危险的环境中，中提琴的生命受到了威胁-而且当托德发誓要保护她时，他将不得不发掘自己的内在力量并解开地球的黑暗秘密。
    明星云集的演员还包括Mads Mikkelson（奇异博士）饰演David Prentiss。尼克·乔纳斯（尼克·纳纳斯（Jumanji：Welcome to the Jungle and the Next Level）），戴维·普伦蒂斯（Davy Prentiss）；大卫·奥耶洛（David Oyelowo）（塞尔玛）为牧师亚伦；辛西娅·埃里沃（Cynthia Erivo）（哈丽雅特）饰演希尔迪（Hildy），他是一个名为Farbranch的和平解决方案的领导人。还有库尔特·萨特（无政府主义之子）和迪米安·比希尔（The Heat，Alien：Covenant）作为托伦的养父西里安和本。
 虽然最初定于2019年3月发布，但Lionsgate担心测试筛选不佳，并下令重新拍摄。鉴于里德利和荷兰分别对《星球大战：天行者的崛起》和《蜘蛛侠：远离家乡》的承诺，这些重新拍摄直到2019年4月才发生。传统上，重新拍摄被视为生产有问题的迹象：X战警：《黑暗凤凰》和《新突变》是最近的主要例子。但也有明显的例外。希望“混沌行走”就是这样，Lionsgate希望它将推出一个新的系列。
 《混沌行走》定于2021年1月22日在美国发行。Lionsgate是否还会包括VOD或类似的流媒体选项？就剧院的重新开放而言，我们将不得不看看接下来几个月的情况如何。
note_en:
A young man and woman on a distant planet called the New World find themselves on the run from a town of religious fanatics in   Chaos Walking, a forthcoming film directed by Doug Liman and based on the  award-winning sci-fi trilogy of the same name by Patrick Ness. And the film boasts two megastars: Daisy Ridley, who plays Rey in the most recent   Star Wars trilogy, and Tom Holland, the latest incarnation of  Spider-Man in the  MCEU.
 The series consists of three novels— The Knife of Letting Go,  The Ask and the Answer, and  Monsters of Men—as well as three short stories set in the same fictional universe, intended as companion pieces to the novels. The series takes place on a planet called New World, where we meet Todd, a boy living in a religiously devoted settlement known as Prentisstown, led by the megalomaniac mayor, David Prentiss. There are no women left, and all the men are afflicted by something called the "Noise," which makes their thoughts audible, driving many of them mad. The same is true for Todd's little dog, Manchee. This is purportedly the result of biological warfare on the part of a native intelligent species known as Spackle, who resented the arrival of the colonizers. The germ killed all the women and left the men with the Noise.
 One day, Todd discovers a patch of silence (a "hole in the Noise") in a nearby swamp, and when he tells his adoptive parents about it, they insist he has to flee Prentisstown. Back in the swamp, Todd comes face to face with Viola, a young girl who has crash-landed on New World in a small scouting craft, ahead of an incoming ship of new planetary settlers. They are relentlessly pursued by the mayor, his son Davy, and an evil preacher named Aaron as they seek refuge with other, more peaceful settlements—including a town called Haven that is rumored to have a cure for the Noise—in hopes of finding a way to warn the incoming ship of the potential violence they face.
  The trilogy's title is taken from a key line in the first book: "The Noise is a man unfiltered, and without a filter, a man is just chaos walking." Ness  has said that he got the idea for the trilogy while pondering our current information overload and its impact on young people in particular, as well as his frustration that talking dogs in fiction "never actually talk like... I think dogs would actually talk." Thematically, he describes the first book as being about flight, the second about tyranny, and the third about war.
    Charlie Kaufman penned a first draft of the script but left the project soon after. Numerous script revisions followed, with Ness himself contributing to the effort. (He is also the author of  A Monster Calls and wrote the script for that film adaptation.) Then Liman signed on to direct, with Ridley and Holland on board as co-stars. The film was shot in Montreal, Scotland, and Iceland.
 Liman's film looks to follow the first book pretty closely, judging from the trailer, although Todd and Viola are obviously much older in the film (Todd is just about to turn 13 in the first novel). Per the official premise:
 In the not too distant future, Todd Hewitt (Tom Holland) discovers Viola (Daisy Ridley), a mysterious girl who crash lands on his planet, where all the women have disappeared and the men are afflicted by “the Noise”—a force that puts all their thoughts on display. In this dangerous landscape, Viola's life is threatened—and as Todd vows to protect her, he will have to discover his own inner power and unlock the planet's dark secrets.
    The star-studded cast also includes Mads Mikkelson (  Doctor Strange) as David Prentiss; Nick Jonas ( Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle and   The Next Level) as Davy Prentiss; David Oyelowo ( Selma) as the priest Aaron; Cynthia Erivo ( Harriet) as Hildy, leader of a peaceful settlement called Farbranch; and Kurt Sutter ( Sons of Anarchy) and Demián Bichir ( The Heat,  Alien: Covenant) as Cillian and Ben, Todd's adoptive fathers.
 While it was originally slated for a March 2019 release, Lionsgate  was concerned about the poor test screenings and ordered reshoots. Those reshoots didn't happen until April 2019, given Ridley's and Holland's commitments to   Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker and   Spider-Man: Far From Home, respectively. Reshoots are traditionally seen as an indication that a production is in trouble:   X-Men: Dark Phoenix and  The New Mutants are prime recent examples. But there are also notable exceptions. Here's hoping that's the case for  Chaos Walking, which Lionsgate hopes will launch a new franchise.
 Chaos Walking is slated for US release on January 22, 2021. Will Lionsgate also include a VOD or similar streaming option? We'll have to see how things shake out over the next couple of months, in terms of theater reopenings.

今天，该公司吹嘘称，Facebook的软件系统在检测和阻止仇恨言论方面变得越来越出色，该公司今天吹嘘着它，但是最艰巨的工作仍然必须由人们来完成，其中许多人警告说，这是世界上最大的社交媒体公司使他们处于不安全的工作环境中。
 Facebook在其最新的社区标准执行报告中说，Facebook上约有95％的仇恨言论在被任何人举报之前就已经被算法捕获。在过去的一个季度中，约有2200万个已标记帖子中的其余5％由用户报告。
  该报告还跟踪了一种新的仇恨言论指标：患病率。基本上，为了衡量患病率，Facebook会对内容进行抽样，然后查找他们衡量的事物（本例中为仇恨言论）被视为查看内容百分比的频率。在今年7月至9月之间，该数字在0.10％至0.11％之间，即每10,000个观看次数中有10-11个观看。
 Facebook在新闻发布会和新闻发布会中都强调，尽管其内部AI在几类内容执行方面取得了长足进步，但COVID-19对其内容审核的能力产生了持续影响。
 该公司表示：“尽管COVID-19大流行继续破坏我们的内容审核人员队伍，但我们仍看到一些执法指标恢复到大流行前水平。” “即使审查能力有所降低，我们仍然会优先考虑最敏感的内容以供人们审查，其中包括自杀，自伤和儿童裸体等领域。”
Facebook诚信副总裁盖伊·罗森（Guy Rosen）在电话中对评论家至关重要。他说：“人们是内容实施等式的重要组成部分。” “这些都是非常重要的工人，他们在这项工作中发挥着非常重要的作用。”
 该公司本身雇用的全职Facebook员工被告知在家中工作到2021年7月，甚至可能是永久性的。
 在与记者的电话会议上，罗森强调说，要求Facebook员工从事实际工作的员工（例如管理数据中心基本功能的员工）必须配备严格的安全预防措施和个人防护设备，例如洗手液，提供。
 罗森说，节制是不能总是在家完成的工作之一。他解释说，有些内容太敏感了，无法在其他家庭成员可能看到的专用工作区之外进行审查，他说，一些Facebook内容主持人正被带回办公室，“以确保我们能够在工作的人和AI之间保持平衡那些需要人工判断的区域”。
 但是，Facebook的大多数内容主持人不适用于Facebook。他们为世界各地的第三方合同公司工作，而他们的工作经常受到严重支持的不足。来自《卫报》，《濒临灭绝》，《华盛顿邮报》和BuzzFeed News等的记者与世界各地的这些合同工进行了交谈，他们描述了无情的期望和工作中普遍的创伤。今年早些时候，Facebook同意由前内容主持人提起的集体诉讼中的5200万美元和解，他们指控这项工作使他们“伤了”创伤后应激障碍。
所有这些都是在COVID-19散布到世界各地之前。面对大流行，情况看起来更加糟糕。被告知要回到办公室的200多位主持人在一封公开信上签名，指责Facebook“不必要地冒着主持人的生命危险”，甚至没有为被下令送回办公室的工人提供危险津贴。
 信中写道：“现在，在具有心理毒性的工作之上，坚持这份工作意味着走进了一个热区。” “在几个办公室里，发生了多个COVID案件。工人们要求Facebook领导层以及埃森哲和CPL等外包公司的领导层采取紧急步骤保护我们并重视我们的工作。您拒绝了。我们正在发布这封信是因为我们别无选择。”
 这封信补充说：“这提出了一个严峻的问题。” “如果我们的工作对Facebook的业务如此核心，以至于您会要求我们以Facebook社区的名义和利益来冒生命危险，那么，我们实际上不是您公司的核心吗？”
  同时，州和联邦对Facebook的审查也在不断增长。本周，公司首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）在短短三周内第二次在参议院作证。众议院议员还抱怨说，由于与选举有关的虚假信息泛滥，Facebook无法适当或安全地管理内容。
  其他监管机构很可能会很快加入Facebook。据媒体报道，从2019年开始的许多反托拉斯调查都已接近尾声。据报道，联邦贸易委员会计划在接下来的两周内提起诉讼，由纽约州总检察长莱蒂西亚·詹姆斯领导的近40个州的联盟很可能会在12月提出诉讼。这些诉讼可能会争辩说，Facebook通过收购和数据策略不公平地扼杀了竞争，最终可能试图迫使该公司剥离Instagram和WhatsApp。
note_en:
Facebook's software systems get ever better at detecting and blocking hate speech on both the Facebook and Instagram platforms, the company boasted today—but the hardest work still has to be done by people, and many of those people warn that the world's biggest social media company is putting them in unsafe working conditions.
 About 95 percent of hate speech on Facebook gets caught by algorithms before anyone can report it, Facebook said in its  latest community-standards enforcement report. The remaining 5 percent of the roughly 22 million flagged posts in the past quarter were reported by users.
  That report is also tracking a new hate-speech metric: prevalence. Basically, to measure prevalence, Facebook takes  a sample of content and then looks for how often the thing they're measuring—in this case, hate speech—gets seen as a percentage of  viewed content. Between July and September of this year, the figure was between 0.10 percent and 0.11 percent, or about 10-11 views of every 10,000.
 Facebook also stressed—in both its news release and in a call with press—that while its in-house AI is  making strides in several categories of content enforcement, COVID-19 is having a continued effect on its ability to moderate content.
 "While the COVID-19 pandemic continues to disrupt our content-review workforce, we are seeing some enforcement metrics return to pre-pandemic levels," the company said. "Even with a reduced review capacity, we still prioritize the most sensitive content for people to review, which includes areas like suicide and self-injury and child nudity."
     The reviewers are critical, Facebook Vice President of Integrity Guy Rosen told press in a call. "People are an important part of the equation for content enforcement," he said. "These are incredibly important workers who do an incredibly important part of the job."
 Full-time Facebook employees who are employed by the company itself are being told to work from home  until July 2021 or perhaps even  permanently.
 In the call with reporters, Rosen stressed that Facebook employees who are required to come in to work physically, such as those who manage essential functions in data centers, are being brought in with strict safety precautions and personal protective equipment, such as hand sanitizer, made available.
 Moderation, Rosen said, is one of those jobs that can't always be done at home. Some content is simply too sensitive to review outside of a dedicated workspace where other family members might see it, he explained, saying that some Facebook content moderators are being brought back into offices "to ensure we can have that balance of people and AI working on those areas" that need human judgement applied.
 The majority of Facebook's content moderators, however, do not work for Facebook. They work for third-party contract firms worldwide, often with woefully insufficient support to do their jobs. Reporters from  The Guardian,  The Verge,  The Washington Post, and  BuzzFeed News, among others, have spoken to these contract workers around the world, who  describe relentless expectations and widespread trauma at work. Earlier this year, Facebook agreed to a $52 million settlement in  a class-action suit filed by former content moderators who alleged the job gave them "debilitating" post-traumatic stress disorder.
     All of that was  before COVID-19  spread around the world. In the face of the pandemic, the situation looks even worse. More than 200 moderators who are being told to go back into the office signed on to an  open letter accusing Facebook of "needlessly risking moderators' lives" without even providing hazard pay for workers who are being ordered back into the office.
 "Now, on top of work that is psychologically toxic, holding onto the job means walking into a hot zone," the letter reads. "In several offices, multiple COVID cases have occurred on the floor. Workers have asked Facebook leadership, and the leadership of your outsourcing firms like Accenture and CPL, to take urgent steps to protect us and value our work. You refused. We are publishing this letter because we are left with no choice."
 "This raises a stark question," the letter adds. "If our work is so core to Facebook's business that you will ask us to risk our lives in the name of Facebook's community—and profit—are we not, in fact, the heart of your company?"
  Meanwhile, state and federal scrutiny of Facebook only keeps growing. This week, company CEO Mark Zuckerberg  testified before the Senate for the  second time in just three weeks. Members of the House are  also complaining that Facebook has failed to moderate content properly or safely amid  rampant election-related disinformation.
  Other regulatory bodies are likely coming for Facebook—and soon. Many of the  antitrust investigations that began in 2019 are drawing to a conclusion, according to media reports. The Federal Trade Commission is  reportedly planning to file a suit within the next two weeks, and a coalition of nearly 40 states, led by New York Attorney General Letitia James, is  likely to follow in December. Those suits are likely to argue that Facebook unfairly stifles competition through its acquisition and data strategies, and it may end up trying to force the company to divest Instagram and WhatsApp.

《星际迷航：发现号》是原始系列的前传，大约在柯克船长和他的船员接管美国海军“企业号”并大胆地走到以前没有人去的地方之前十年。但是我们现在处在ST：Disco S3的未知领域，该公司在未来900年的时间里轰炸了该船及其船员。在重新塑造特许经营的未来时，要想忠于特许经营的精神，这构成了相当大的创造性挑战，这不仅是作家，而且是系列道具大师马里奥·莫雷拉（Mario Moreira）和科学顾问艾琳·麦克唐纳（Erin MacDonald）都面临的挑战。
  该系列由Sonequa Martin-Green饰演Michael Burnham，迈克尔·伯纳姆（Michael Burnham）是孤零零的人类，由萨瑞克（James Frain）和他的人类妻子阿曼达·格雷森（Mia Kirshner）（又名Spock的父母，伊桑·佩克（Ethan Peck））抚养长大。因此，她是Spock的养女。正如我之前所写的，S2整个赛季的弧度涉及“红色天使”和流氓Starfleet AI（称为Control）的神秘面纱，该AI试图消灭宇宙中的所有有情生活。
  最大的启示是，《红色天使》实际上是迈克尔的亲生母亲穿着的旅行套装。她意外地跳入了一个凄凉的未来950年，在那之前，Control已实现了其邪恶的目标，并穿越了时光留下信号（红色天使的愿景），希望改变这一未来。在S2结局中，迈克尔穿上了母亲的衣服的副本，以带领“发现”号在900年后的未来。该企业的船员告诉《星际舰队》，发现号在战斗中被摧毁，并被勒令不再谈论该船或其船员。
 在S3揭幕战中，迈克尔（以及后来的发现者和她的船员）到达未来，并发现Control的计划遭到了挫败：生命仍然存在。但是银河系与之不同，这要归功于一个叫做“烧伤”的事件，这是一场灾难性事件，导致银河系中的所有二锂都在此过程中爆炸并摧毁了许多星际舰队。后果是，无法进行翘曲驱动，所有行星都已断开连接，不再受联邦管辖。但是，迈克尔确实在新盟友布克（David Ajala）的帮助下，在一个偏远的空间站上找到了一个联邦独立联络人。
  根据莫雷拉的说法，“发现”的前两个季节类似于时期作品，因为它们设定在TOS之前10年。这意味着对很多道具进行逆向工程，例如移相器，三阶和通信设备。 （开篇的致谢是对丰富的特许经营历史的致敬。）尽管有标志性的《星际迷航》技术得以保留，但在未来900多年的时间里，Moreira和他的团队变得更加富有创造力。
对于未来技术，逐渐出现了总体设计原则：Moreira和他的团队没有使用多种工具，而是设想了以多功能工具为标准的未来。莫雷拉告诉Ars，因此，新的Federation Tricon徽章是“轮船系统的枢纽”。它们既具有通信功能，又具有运输功能，并且还可以链接到三阶订单，所有这些都可以在一个简单，紧凑的设备中完成。同上一种新的医疗工具，该工具最初在作者房间被昵称为“ Beyond One”，直到每个人最终都称其为“医疗PIN”（复合接种器）。
    莫雷拉说：“这是你的三阶仪，是麻醉剂，是激光手术刀。” “这一切都是合一的，因为将来的一切都是要减少到可以完成最大工作量的最小项目。”
 本季（迄今为止）最大的技术飞跃是“可编程物质”的引入，它基本上是当今纳米技术和3D打印功能的高度先进版本。可编程物质几乎可以采取任何形式：可以是固体，液体甚至全息照相。这是Book船用控制台的关键功能，但在多功能工具方面也是一个巨大的优势。
 莫雷拉说：“携带在人身上的预先编程的物质可以变成预先编程的形状。” “您总是可以使用自己的工具，”但是您不必携带许多单独的工具。
 他的团队还重新设计了标志性的“星际迷航”移相器作为可编程物质单元。莫雷拉说：“ ST：TNG的上一个移相器因其形状而被粉丝戏称为“海豚”。 “我们知道我们想要比这更坚强。我们已经给它起了绰号'大白鲨'，因此我们拥有鲨鱼身体形状的元素。”
莫雷拉（Moreira）特别为第一集中的手炮感到骄傲（“那是您的希望之一”）。他说：“我们受到了警卫们启用腕部技术的想法的启发，当他们的移相器问世时，它将意识到腕部的形状。”由于最近在涂料和3D打印方面的进步，“您在屏幕上看到的所有手动大炮都是泡沫特技版本和安全（'英雄'）版本，”莫雷拉（Moreira）说。 “没有必要来回切换。”
 在S3的第五集（“尝试死亡”）中，发现者终于与处于医疗危机中的Starfleet的残余人员团聚。急于向可疑的星际舰队司令万斯（Oded Fehr），伯纳姆，休博士（威尔逊·克鲁兹）和安全官员纳恩（Rachael Ancheril）证明其有用性和可信赖性，利用发现号的孢子驱动器前往季霍夫号航空母舰。蒂霍夫（Tikhov）拥有种子档案，这些档案可能会治愈所有破坏Starfleet的疾病。
 种子档案的概念在挪威的斯瓦尔巴群岛全球种子库中具有现实世界的对应之处，这为莫雷拉的设计提供了许多灵感。在斯瓦尔巴德群岛，种子被存储在三层密封的铝箔包装中，然后将其放入冷藏室中金属货架上的塑料手提袋容器中。为了更新该系列的概念，莫雷拉说：“我们设计了可以在内部容纳液体的单单元制冷件。”它看起来像是银行保险库中一个未来派的高科技保险箱。
     对于科学顾问天文学家艾琳·麦克唐纳（Erin MacDonald）而言，他正在《星际迷航》上工作：探索是梦想成真。 （她还就《极地争霸》的三集进行过咨询。）她经常引用《星际迷航：旅行者》的凯瑟琳·珍妮威上尉，激发她与《 X档案》的达娜·史卡利一起从事科学事业。因此，在第5集中看到USS Voyager的升级后代版本特别令人兴奋。
 尽管星际迷航采用了未来主义的设计，但它对整合尖端科学和技术的方式始终采取相当认真的态度，这就是许多像MacDonald这样的工作科学家将其对特许经营权的热爱视为他们选择制造该特许权的主要原因之一。科学他们的职业。
麦克唐纳告诉Ars：“由于《星际迷航》，我们经历了几代人进入科学的时代，我认为很多创造者和作家都希望保持这一遗产。”
 像“发现”这样的好莱坞系列电影所面临的挑战是娱乐性，同时还要包括可以启发后代的炫酷，坚实的科学。这是一个微妙的平衡，最终故事是第一位的。这就意味着，当叙述需要时，有时愿意在合理性方面扩展科学。麦克唐纳说：“只要这是一个有意识的决定，并且您希望将其纳入考虑范围，就可以围绕科学开展工作。”
 S3保持科学合理准确度的最成功例子之一，尽管仍然为故事服务，但迈克尔在未来的一年比发现号船员提前了整整一年，尽管他们相距仅一分钟左右就进入了虫洞。 。这是引力时间膨胀的一个例子，这是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的相对论（麦克唐纳的科学专业之一）的相对论的主要特征。爱因斯坦将空间的三个维度与时间的第四维度统一起来，并且这种时空将在存在质量或等效变形的情况下发生扭曲和拉伸。麦克唐纳说：“因此，当你在引力井内时，与在引力井外的人所经历的时间是不同的。”
  这种特殊的衬套是《星际迷航》系列中的常见功能。麦克唐纳说：“在《 ST：发现》中，“当他们经过虫洞时，时空就变得极其漫长。” “因此，发现号仍在虫洞中，工作人员所经历的时间与伯纳姆不同，伯纳姆率先出局，因此不在虫洞中。这是实施某些已知物理学并将其极端化为一种很好的方法。讲故事的工具。”
 麦当劳的影响力在第五集中尤为明显，迈克尔和她的同伙到达蒂霍夫（Tikhov），发现除了一个异相的男人以外，所有乘员都已死亡。他们了解到，致命事件是日冕物质抛射（CME），它是从太阳等恒星中释放出大量等离子体，当它们撞击地球的磁层时会引起地磁风暴。 （地球上记录到的最大太阳风暴发生在1859年，被称为卡灵顿事件。它摧毁了电报系统，并伴有强烈的极光显示。）蒂霍夫号上唯一的幸存者正好是在中途传送到种子库的时候一阵辐射袭来。因此他幸存下来，尽管陷入了异相。
麦克唐纳说：“我们都喜欢我们的运输事故，所以这只是书本上的又一个伟大事件。”
 到目前为止，尽管粉丝理论比比皆是，但《星际迷航：发现》仍保留有关《伯恩》的细节的更多细节。那么，麦克唐纳（MacDonald）是否对造成“烧伤”的原因有任何科学见解？ “无可奉告。”她笑了。 “我说的任何话都将是一个明确的答案。”她的建议：“敬请期待。”
 《星际迷航》的新剧集：《发现之旅》第三季每周四在CBS All Access播出。
note_en:
Star Trek: Discovery started out as a prequel to the original series, set roughly 10 years before Captain Kirk and his crew took over the USS  Enterprise and boldly went where no man had gone before. But we're now in uncharted territory with  ST: Disco S3, which rocketed the ship and her crew over 900 years into the future. That posed a considerable creative challenge to stay true to the ethos of the franchise while reimagining its future—a challenge facing not just the writers, but series prop master Mario Moreira and science consultant Erin MacDonald as well.
  The series stars Sonequa Martin-Green as Michael Burnham, an orphaned human raised on the planet Vulcan by none other than Sarek (James Frain) and his human wife, Amanda Grayson (Mia Kirshner)—aka, Spock's (Ethan Peck) parents. So she is Spock's adoptive sister. As  I've written previously, the S2 season-long arc involved the mysterious appearances of a "Red Angel" and a rogue Starfleet AI called Control that sought to wipe out all sentient life in the universe.
  The big reveal was that the Red Angel was actually a time-travel suit worn by Michael's biological mother. She had accidentally jumped 950 years into a bleak future in which Control had achieved its nefarious goal and had been traveling through time leaving signals (the Red Angel visions), hoping to alter that future. In the S2 finale, Michael donned a copy of her mother's suit to lead  Discovery over 900 years into the future. The crew of the  Enterprise told Starfleet that  Discovery was destroyed in the battle and were ordered to never speak of the ship or her crew again.
 In the S3 opener, Michael (and, eventually,  Discovery and her crew) arrives in the future and finds that Control's plan has been thwarted: life still exists. But the galaxy is very different thanks to something called The Burn, a catastrophic event that caused all the dilithium in the Milky Way to explode and destroy much of Starfleet in the process. In the aftermath, with no warp drive possible, all the planets have become disconnected and are no longer governed by the Federation. Michael does, however, manage to locate one sole Federation liaison on a remote space station, with the help of a new ally, Book (David Ajala).
  The first two seasons of  Discovery were akin to period pieces, according to Moreira, since they were set 10 years before  TOS. That meant reverse engineering a lot of the props, like the phasers, tricorders, and communication devices. (The  opening credits pay homage to that  rich franchise history.) Jumping 900+ years into the future freed Moreira and his team to be a bit more creative, although the iconic   Star Trek technologies are still preserved.
 For the futuristic tech, an overall design principle did gradually emerge: rather than having many different kinds of tools, Moreira and his team envisioned a future where the multipurpose tool was the norm. So the new Federation Tricon badges are "hubs to the ship's systems," Moreira told Ars. They serve both communication and transporter functions and can link to tricorders as well, all in one simple, compact device. Ditto for the new medical tool, which was initially nicknamed "Beyond One" in the writers room until everyone eventually settled on calling it the "medical PIN" (poly-inoculator).
    "It's your tricorder, it's an anesthetic, it's a laser scalpel," said Moreira. "It's everything in one, because everything in the future is about getting down to a minimal item that can do the maximum amount of work."
 The biggest technology leap this season (thus far) was the introduction of "programmable matter," which is basically a highly advanced version of the nanotechnology and 3D-printing capabilities of today. Programmable matter can take almost any form: it can be solid, liquid, or even holographic. It's a key feature of the control console for Book's ship, but it's also a huge advantage when it comes to multipurpose tools.
 "A pre-programmed amount of matter that's carried on a person can become a pre-programmed shape," said Moreira. "You'd always have your tools on you," but you wouldn't need to be carrying a number of individual tools.
 His team also redesigned the iconic  Star Trek phasers as programmable matter units. "The previous phaser on  ST: TNG was nicknamed 'the Dolphin' by fans because of its shape," Moreira said. "We knew we wanted ours to be tougher than that. We had already nicknamed it the 'Great White Shark,' so we have elements of the shape of a shark body.'
    Moreira is especially proud of the hand cannons featured in the first episode ("That Hope Is You Part 1"). "We were inspired by the idea that the guards would have this wrist tech on, and when their phasers came out, it would realize the shape of their wrist," he said. And thanks to recent advances in paint finishes and 3D printing, "All those hand cannons you saw on-screen were both foam stunt versions and safe ('hero') versions," said Moreira. "There was no need to switch back and forth."
 In the fifth S3 episode ("Die Trying"), the people of  Discovery are finally reunited with the remnants of Starfleet, which is in the midst of a medical crisis. Anxious to prove their usefulness (and trustworthiness) to a suspicious Starfleet Commander-in-Chief Vance (Oded Fehr), Burnham, Dr. Hugh (Wilson Cruz), and security officer Nahn (Rachael Ancheril) use   Discovery's spore drive to travel to the USS  Tikhov. The  Tikhov houses the seed archives that might hold the cure for whatever disease is ravaging Starfleet.
 The concept of the seed archives has a real-world counterpart in the  Svalbard global seed vault in Norway, which provided much of the inspiration for Moreira's design. At Svalbard, seeds are stored in sealed three-ply foil packages, which in turn are placed into plastic tote containers on metal shelving racks in refrigerated rooms. To update the concept for the series, "We designed single-unit refrigeration pieces that could hold liquid inside," said Moreira. It looks like a futuristic, high-tech collection of safety deposit boxes inside a bank vault.
     For science consultant Erin MacDonald, an astrophysicist by training, working on  Star Trek: Discovery is a dream come true. (She has also consulted on three several episodes of   Orbital Redux.) She regularly cites Captain  Kathryn Janeway of   Star Trek: Voyager as inspiring her to pursue a career in science, along with  The X-Files' Dana Scully. So seeing an upgraded, future-generation version of the USS  Voyager in episode 5 was a particular thrill.
 Despite its futuristic setting,  Star Trek has always taken a fairly serious approach to how it incorporates cutting-edge science and technology—that's one reason so many working scientists, like MacDonald, cite their love of the franchise as a main reason they chose to make science their career.
 "We're on multiple generations of people who have gone into science because of  Star Trek, and I think a lot of the creators and writers want to keep that legacy going," MacDonald told Ars.
 The challenge for a Hollywood series like  Discovery is to be entertaining while still including cool, solid science that could inspire future generations. It's a delicate balance, and ultimately the story comes first. That means occasionally being willing to stretch the science a bit in terms of plausibility when the narrative requires it. "As long as it's a conscious decision and you want to have that factored into it, you can work around the science," said MacDonald.
 One of the most successful S3 examples of keeping the science reasonably accurate, while still in service to the story, is when Michael arrives in the future a full year ahead of the  Discovery crew, even though they entered the wormhole just a minute or so apart. It's an example of gravitational time dilation, a central feature of Albert Einstein's general theory of relativity—one of MacDonald's scientific specialties. Einstein unified the three dimensions of space with the fourth dimension of time, and this spacetime will warp and stretch in the presence of a mass or equivalent distortion. "So when you're inside a gravitational well, you experience time differently than someone outside of that gravitational well," said MacDonald.
  This particular trope is a common feature in the  Star Trek franchise. In the case of  ST: Discovery, "As they're going through the wormhole, there is an extreme stretching of spacetime," MacDonald said. "So as  Discovery is still in the wormhole, the crew were experiencing time differently than Burnham, who came out first and [thus] was outside the wormhole. It's a great way of implementing some known physics and taking it to the extreme degree as a storytelling tool."
 MacDonald's influence is particularly evident in the fifth episode, in which Michael and her cohorts arrive at the  Tikhov to find all the occupants are dead except for one man, who exists out of phase. They learn that the fatal event was a  coronal mass ejection (CME), a massive release of plasma from a star like our Sun that can give rise to geomagnetic (Solar) storms when they hit Earth's magnetosphere. (The largest Solar storm recorded on Earth occurred in 1859 and was known as the  Carrington Event. It knocked out telegraph systems and was accompanied by strong  auroral displays.) The sole survivor aboard  Tikhov happened to be mid-teleport into the seed bank when the burst of radiation hit. So he survived, albeit trapped out-of-phase.
 "We all love our transporter accidents," said MacDonald, "So this is just another great one for the books."
 Thus far,  Star Trek: Discovery continues to withhold any further details about the specifics surrounding The Burn, although fan theories abound. So does MacDonald have any scientific insights into what might have caused The Burn? "No comment," she laughed. "Anything I say is going to be a definitive answer." Her advice: "Stay tuned."
 New episodes of  Star Trek: Discovery's third season air every Thursday on CBS All Access.
title: 接受挑战：为ST：Discovery S3创造一个遥远的未来
title_en: Challenge accepted: Inventing a plausible far-flung future for ST: Discovery S3

note:
随着美国进入较冷的月份，并且连续几天破获每日最高记录的COVID-19病例，找到最适合您需求的口罩比以往任何时候都更加重要。虽然戴上总比没有好，但不幸的是，找到符合WHO和CDC准则的口罩并不是一件容易的事或富有成果的事情。满足这些建议并不难，但是针对各种不同需求研究和编译市场上最好的口罩证明很少有制造商这样做。幸运的是，有一些。
 我们已经详细介绍了当前的大流行，如何处理大流行以及如何最好地应对这些大流行时期。希望本文对这些主题有所帮助，特别是关于面罩的最新科学，如何使用它们以及购买时的注意事项。
 根据CDC和WHO的标准，我们还将重点介绍一些选项，这些选项应有助于确保所有人的安全，无论您是室外跑步者，听力障碍者还是仅需要优质可重复使用的口罩。
   作为一种仅在过去一年才被我们了解的疾病，与我们已经研究了数十年甚至数百年的病原体相比，COVID-19的流行病学和病理学仍处于新生阶段。尽管Ars希望我们能为进一步推动这项研究做出贡献，但我们没有（如整个科学界）所希望的那样深入研究各种口罩及其对COVID-19传播的影响的工具。 。因此，我们依赖于最新的科学发现和长期了解的知识，即通常情况下，适当的口罩会限制病原体进入或逃逸可吸收和排出的呼吸系统。
 从历史上看，戴口罩的科学一直致力于对减慢/预防空气传播疾病的传播产生积极影响。对于我们中的某些人来说，这是我们需要知道的，在现在的COVID-19大流行期间，什么时候让公众采取减慢传播的措施。但是，起初，像WHO和CDC这样的主要卫生组织都对推荐通用掩膜感到不安。
总而言之，反对戴眼镜的理由归结为缺乏证据（尽管这不是缺乏证据，因为针对COVID-19社区传播的口罩功效尚待深入研究），医护人员的供应短缺以及担心口罩会提供错误的安全感，从而损害公众对减少传播的更重要方面的依从感，例如社会距离，限制聚会和勤洗手。
 到目前为止，除了维持医护人员的物资外，科学界在很大程度上认为，在公共场所戴口罩的作用克服了这些犹豫，并且是减缓COPID-19的关键组成部分，并且希望这一天能够消除大流行。至于唯一剩下的问题，就是医护人员短缺，这就是使用布口罩的原因。
   根据WHO面膜指南，如果您有COVID-19，COVID-19症状，正在照顾可能感染的人或易感人群（≥60岁，具有潜在合并症，例如心血管疾病或糖尿病）细菌，慢性肺部疾病，癌症，脑血管疾病或免疫抑制），那么最安全的选择就是保护自己免受病毒侵袭或控制传播（如果有的话），例如N95或KN95。
 同样，如果您与有或可能有COVID-19的人一起居住或正在照顾患者，则这些医用口罩是唯一合适的设备。与患者密切接触时，强烈建议同时使用可清洗的面罩，以提高安全性并延长面罩的有效性和完整性。佩戴时，在N95或KN95的外部放置外科口罩也有助于保护它。还应使用社交隔离和经常洗手。有关护理COVID-19患者的更多信息，请查阅CDC的指南。
  但是，如果您不属于这些情况之一，则应使用正确指定的非医疗口罩，以及与社会保持距离。
在这种情况下，“诀窍”是限制您对病毒的暴露（保护）和传播（源代码控制）病毒的能力。最终，通过与他人保持至少6英尺的距离，戴上脸罩并避免身体不适，可以极大地降低感染您（或使您感染他人）所需的临界暴露量或病毒载量的可能性。通风的室内环境。
  对于这种情况下的健康个体，不符合N95过滤标准的口罩似乎可以有效地保护人们的日常必需任务。您可以在深入研究口罩数据时阅读有关这些研究的更多信息，但仅需说一下，在室外通风良好的环境中，遵循WHO / CDC建议的布口罩就足以确保您和他人的安全。
  鉴于全球医疗口罩的持续短缺，织物口罩的广泛普及（因此采用率更高）以及这些口罩的可清洗性和可重复使用性，这意味着，口罩通常是健康公众的最佳选择。
 如果您需要KN95或N95口罩，请记住全局短缺，考虑到您的需求和使用习惯，并做出相应的判断。换句话说，如果您不是一个高风险人士，并且经常在公共场所工作，那么购买许多医用口罩的可持续性将大大增加医护人员已经很严重的PPE（个人防护设备）可用性随着大流行继续增加，患者和弱势人群。
 如果您处在像我这样的情况下-我有高风险的家庭成员，因此在公众场合露面的机会很少，以至于平均来说，六个KN95口罩可以让我整整一年（每次使用不超过五次，不得超过根据CDC针对卫生保健工作者的危机级别短缺规定，每个口罩上总共需要使用8个小时），那么也许这对您也是可行的选择。非N95或同等级别的外科口罩由于其配件和较低的过滤水平，未获CDC批准重复使用。
当然，根据您的需要，医用口罩和织物口罩也有优势。当然，布口罩的长期可重复使用性是它们的最大优势。为了确保长期安全，这些口罩应具有一些关键功能。
   购买优质的布口罩时，世卫组织和疾病预防控制中心提出了一些需要注意的事项。这是摘要。
  寻找具有至少三层的蒙版，如下所述-越多越好。合成非织造材料的疏水中间层，例如聚丙烯或其他棉层，可以增强过滤或保留液滴。
 确保良好的密封性。鼻梁中的形状配合线特别有用。
 确保它紧贴但舒适地贴在脸部两侧，并完全覆盖鼻子和嘴巴
选择可以清洗和机干而不损坏或变形的材料。
  请勿在面罩的任何层上使用涂有蜡或其他类似化合物的织物，因为它们会阻塞面罩的毛孔以使空气流通，从而在面罩侧面产生更多的空气通过。
 不要使用可拉伸的织物制成的口罩，因为它们在拉伸时会增加孔径。
 遵守这些准则将有助于确保您自己和他人的功效，可重复使用性和安全性。
  戴上或戴上干净的口罩是一个相对简单的过程。首先，洗手至少20秒钟，然后握住领带或耳圈，将面罩戴在脸上，并确保鼻梁，脸颊，下巴和脸部周围良好密封。
戴上口罩时，请勿触摸口罩的前部，因为表面上可能有病毒颗粒。到现在为止，您可能已经听说过不要触摸脸部（或鼻子，嘴巴和眼睛），以免因病毒的泛滥传播而造成潜在的感染，这种感染是通过接触可能被其污染的表面并将其引入人体而引起的。戴上口罩时，应特别注意将其正面视为潜在的有害物质（传播源），因此在戴上口罩时应确保正确佩戴，以免戴上时接触它。
     当您脱下口罩或“脱口罩”时，这种情况还会继续。仅抓住耳朵的环或系带，解开它们，然后从您的脸部向下取下面罩，注意避免与面罩的前部接触。戴上口罩后立即洗净；可以与其他耐高温耐洗衣服放在一起（在下一节中有更多介绍）。如果您不能立即清洗口罩，请确保将其向内折叠，使内部（面对您的脸）朝外，然后将其放在单个一次性袋中，直到可以清洗为止。脱下口罩后，立即洗手至少20秒钟。
 在整个穿戴过程中，您不应多次穿戴和脱下口罩。每次使用后都应清洗或消毒口罩。因此，有多个适合您佩戴习惯的口罩会很有帮助，因此您永远都不会戴上受污染的口罩。任何暴露在外部液体中或其他损坏的口罩都应在安全的情况下尽快取下并丢弃。如果织物层看起来明显磨损，或您发现有孔洞，请丢弃面罩。
  将面罩固定在脸上，并确保贴合舒适，透气并具有最佳密封效果。
 佩戴时请勿触摸面罩的前部，或在佩戴过程中多次将其摘下。
从领带或耳圈上取下口罩，然后将其直接放在洗漱液中。
  脱下并清洗/储存口罩后，至少要洗手20秒钟。
   一般来说，您应该遵循口罩制造商提供的洗涤或消毒说明。不要将口罩放在微波炉，烤箱中，或尝试使用紫外线对口罩进行消毒。这些不是杀死面具上所有细菌的安全或有效方法。当前市场上没有可实现任何类型口罩灭菌的消费产品-发射UVC，辐射或其他方式。
 如果您没有口罩的清洗说明，可以按照以下一般准则安全有效地清洗织物口罩。
  由非织造聚丙烯纺粘织物与棉结合而成的口罩可以承受高温，因此通常可以蒸或煮。
如果没有热水，请用肥皂或清洁剂和室温的水清洗，然后将口罩煮沸一分钟（如果使用适当的材料制成）或将其浸入0.1％的氯气中一分钟，然后彻底冲洗。用室温水避免氯残留有毒。
  使用含有5.25％至8.25％的次氯酸钠的漂白剂。请勿在此范围以外或未指定范围的情况下使用漂白剂。
 检查标签，看漂白剂是否打算消毒。某些漂白产品（例如为颜色安全使用而制造的产品）无法满足消毒需求。
    通过混合制备漂白剂溶液：每加仑的室温水5汤匙（1/3杯）的漂白剂为5.25％– 8.25％
 或4茶匙每夸脱室温水漂白5.25％– 8.25％的漂白剂
将溶液倒入下水道，并用冷水或室温水彻底冲洗面罩。
   清洗后，请确保检查面罩是否有损坏或形成孔的迹象。像任何织物一样，随着时间的流逝，它会开始降解。在这里保持警惕会让您知道何时该购买新面罩，尤其是在制造商未声明预计使用寿命的情况下。
  如果您需要使用KN95或N95口罩，则可以将它们重复使用最多五次使用，根据为大流行情况下的卫生保健专业人员设计的CDC指南，这些口罩的总使用时间不得超过八小时。研究表明，经过五次使用，面罩的完整性（尤其是其佩戴状态）已下降到超过安全使用点的程度。总使用时间超过8小时的情况相同。为确保完整性，用户应在每次重复使用时进行密封检查。
 如果您遵循这些步骤，则强烈建议采取轮换政策，每次使用之间至少间隔一周，同时将其存储在单独的透气纸袋中。这有助于限制从受污染的面罩传播污染物的可能性，同时扩大N95或类似产品的使用范围。
 医用口罩的使用期限不算高，因为它们制造一次只能使用一次，一旦取下即丢弃。但是CDC指出，遵循这些准则以及上面概述的安全脱除PPE的准则，可以使用户在处置前多次安全使用同一口罩。
寻找用于消毒一次性口罩的方法将带来一些结果，包括FDA批准的（通过紧急使用授权）和CDC / NIOSH（国家职业安全与健康研究所）认可的卫生保健设施的方法，尤其是面部危机级别的呼吸器短缺。
 至关重要的是，只有某些医疗保健机构才具有适当的工具，可以有效地使用这些经过最少研究的策略来消毒一次性医用口罩。这些规定不仅需要特定的实验室级工具和严格的应用，而且是为在最坏情况下短缺的情况下使用而准备的，在这种情况下，医护人员必须使用此类方法为自己提供一定程度的保护，以防已知的感染者。底线：请勿尝试在家中（或在公共场所）模仿，因为您做不到。
 如我们先前所述，目前市场上没有可实现任何类型的口罩灭菌的消费产品-发射UVC，辐射或其他方式。这使可洗布口罩对公众具有巨大优势。
   限制COVID-19传播的最佳口罩是N95呼吸器。当我们说“最好的全方位”时，这意味着对大多数人（尤其是健康，低风险的人）最好的面具。
  （Ars Technica可能通过会员计划通过此帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。）
肯尼斯·科尔的优质纯棉口罩之所以成为我们最重要的全面建议，是因为它不仅具有CDC和WHO的最佳实践，而且具有超越其最佳实践的罕见特征。这些口罩由六层组成，这些层包括一个内部棉层，四个非织造过滤层和一个防潮抗菌外层，它们可以检查所有箱子，其中一些会被多次检查。正如您在吹捧此功能的任何面罩上所发现的那样，该抗菌剂目前尚未获得杀死SARS-CoV-2病毒的认证，但已通过EPA注册，在140°F的温度下至少可洗涤30次。
 专门处理织物和纺织品的化学制造公司HeiQ说，这种涂层将在30分钟内杀死99.9％的病毒和细菌。尽管尚未证明该涂层能特异性杀死SARS-CoV-2，但这应有助于避免气味并可能进一步提高对病原体的防御能力。
 口罩也可机洗，并带有鼻夹，可沿着鼻梁和脸颊贴合。这对于任何戴口罩的人都是建议的，但是对于戴眼镜的人尤其有用，因为这种更好的配合可以防止眼镜起雾。
 肯尼斯·科尔的保费不是唯一值得的口罩，但就布口罩而言，它是CDC和WHO的所有要求的典范。由于它的合身性和可调节性（有助于最大程度地减少空气逸出），我们也推荐此款眼镜为戴眼镜的人最好的面罩，尽管出于相同的原因，此列表中的任何面具也应做得很好。
    （Ars Technica可能通过会员计划通过此帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。）
与在多次清洗过程中依靠口罩过滤器的完整性相比，向布面罩添加可更换的过滤器是一种更好的长期解决方案，可保持较高的过滤完整性。随着任何透气材料的开始磨损，过滤不可避免地会受到影响。尽管您仍应保持警惕使用可更换过滤器的口罩的磨损痕迹，但每次使用都可以更换全新过滤器的能力意味着您不必质疑主过滤层的完整性。我们在这里有两个选择-一个带有一次性过滤器，另一个带有可清洗的过滤器，尽管前者符合更高的过滤标准。 Outdoor Research的Face Mask Kit使用由无纺聚丙烯制成的过滤器，该过滤器与医用口罩使用的材料相同。它们符合ASTM F2100-2019的过滤效率标准，这意味着它们被过滤出的滤料可过滤掉95％以上尺寸为0.1μm或更大的细菌。 SARS-CoV-2病毒颗粒的大小范围从0.5μm到小于0.1μm。 PM2.5过滤器通常在网上出售，这些过滤器对那些对空气污染敏感的人不会很有效，它们只能过滤出最大2.5μm的颗粒。
    ASTM F2100-2019标准还测试了压差（逸出或通过过滤器的空气量），可燃性以及对合成血液渗透的抵抗力。 Outdoor Research的过滤器达到或超过了除血液渗透测试之外的所有条件，该测试专门用于模拟患者的动脉或静脉在面罩上破裂的情况-对于普通大众来说，这几乎是不可能的。
 该公司表示，根据湿度，面部化妆，面部毛发和汗液的不同，其过滤器可使用约五至七次，并且一周最多使用40个小时。根据CDC有关重复使用医用口罩的指南，如果不每天清洗面罩套件，则在两次佩戴之间至少间隔五天使用多个口罩实施轮换政策可能是延长过滤器使用寿命的有用策略，而不是尝试每天都要清洗面膜，然后将部分使用的过滤器转移到刚洗过的面膜中。
 户外研究型口罩应始终在插入过滤器的情况下佩戴，否则口罩只有两层聚酯。尽管这种材料非常适合透气和拒水（世卫组织建议在口罩中使用的两种特质），但如果没有过滤器，仅靠过滤效果就无法提供足够的保护。但是，与一次性过滤器结合使用时，聚酯的水分保持率较低，因此具有较高的静电性能，有助于捕获气溶胶。面罩随附三个过滤器，并且可以相对便宜的三包购买替换品。
 不带过滤器的面罩可在高温下机洗，这是另一个很大的优点，并且经过HeiQ V-Block处理，该面罩可在30分钟内杀死99.9％的病毒和细菌（尽管我们提到过，尚未证实可以杀死SARS-CoV-2）。大约洗涤30次后，这种保护作用开始减弱，但是您不太可能会因此受到很大的影响。无论如何，您都应始终避免触摸面罩的正面。

note_en:
As the United States enters into the colder months and  record-high daily cases of COVID-19 continue to be broken on successive days, finding the best mask for your needs is more important than ever. While wearing something is always better than nothing, unfortunately, finding masks that meet WHO and CDC guidelines isn't a particularly easy or fruitful endeavor. It's not hard to meet these recommendations, but researching and compiling the best masks on the market for a range of different needs proved that few manufacturers do. Fortunately, there are some.
 We've  written at length on the current pandemic, how it's been handled, and how best to handle yourself through these discombobulating times. This article will hopefully serve as a useful refresher on some of those topics, particularly the latest science on masks, how to use them, and what to look for when buying them.
 Based off criteria from the CDC and WHO, we'll also highlight a few options that should help keep everyone safe, whether you're an outdoor runner, hard of hearing, or just in need of a quality reusable mask.
   As a disease that's only come to our knowledge in the past year, the epidemiology and pathology of COVID-19 is still relatively nascent compared to pathogens which we've had decades, or even centuries, to study. As much as we at Ars wish we could contribute to pushing this research further along, we don't have the tools to research various masks and their impact on COVID-19 transmission as deeply as we (and the entire scientific community) would like to. As such, we're reliant on the latest scientific discoveries and the long-understood knowledge that, generally, a proper mask limits pathogens from entering or escaping the respiratory system where they can be absorbed and expelled.
 Historically, the science behind mask-wearing has consistently pointed toward having a positive effect on slowing/preventing transmission of airborne diseases. For some of us, that's all we needed to know when it came time for the public to adopt transmission-slowing measures during the current COVID-19 pandemic. But, at first, leading health organizations like the WHO and CDC both had trepidations about recommending universal masking.
 In summary, the reasoning against wearing them boiled down to absence of evidence (though this is not the evidence of absence, as mask efficacy against COVID-19's community spread has yet to be studied thoroughly), supply shortages for health care workers, and the fear of masks providing a false sense of security to the detriment of the public's adherence to more important aspects of reducing transmission, like social distancing, limiting gatherings, and diligent hand-washing.
 By now, with the exception of maintaining health care workers' supplies, the scientific community largely maintains that the effect of wearing a mask in public overcomes these hesitations and is a crucial component to slowing and—hopefully, one day—eradicating the COVID-19 pandemic. As for the only remaining concern, shortages for health care workers, that's where the use of cloth masks comes in.
   According to  WHO mask guidance, if you have COVID-19, COVID-19 symptoms, are caring for someone who may be infected, or are among the vulnerable population (aged ≥ 60 years, people with underlying comorbidities, such as cardiovascular disease or diabetes mellitus, chronic lung disease, cancer, cerebrovascular disease, or immunosuppression) then a medical mask, such as an N95 or KN95, is the safest bet to protect yourself from the virus or contain the spread if you have it.
 Similarly, if you're in a living space with or are caring for someone who has or may potentially have COVID-19, these medical masks are the only proper equipment. Concurrent use of a cleanable face shield is highly advisable when in close contact with the patient, in an effort to increase your safety and prolong the effectiveness and integrity of your mask. Placing a surgical mask over the outside of the N95 or KN95 while wearing can also help preserve it. Social distancing and frequent hand-washing are also to be employed. For more information on caring for COVID-19 patients, check out  the CDC's guidance.
  But if you're not in one of these situations, properly specified non-medical masks, along with social distancing, should do the trick.
 In this case, "the trick" is limiting your exposure to (protection) and ability to spread (source control) the virus. Ultimately, the potential for reaching a critical amount of exposure, or viral load, needed to infect you (or for you to infect others) is greatly reduced by keeping at least 6 feet of distance from others, wearing a face covering, and avoiding poorly ventilated, indoor environments.
  For healthy individuals in this context, cloth masks that don't meet N95 filtration standards seem to be effective in protecting people during their day-to-day, necessary tasks. You can read more about these studies in  our deep dive on mask data, but suffice it to say, in outdoor, well-ventilated settings, cloth masks that follow the WHO/CDC recommendations should be sufficient to keep yourself and others safe.
  This, taken with the  ongoing global shortage of medical masks, widespread availability of fabric ones (and therefore higher adoption rates), as well as the wash- and reusability of these means that cloth masks are generally the best option for the healthy public.
 If you need KN95 or N95 masks, bear in mind the  global shortage, factor in your needs and use habits, and make that judgement accordingly. In other words, if you're a not a high-risk individual and are in public settings frequently, the sustainability of buying that many medical masks will contribute significantly to the already critical strain on PPE (personal protective equipment) availability for health care workers, patients, and vulnerable populations as the pandemic continues to surge.
 If you're in a situation like mine—I have high-risk family members and therefore have been in public so little that, on average, six KN95 masks last me a full year (using each no more than five times, not to exceed eight hours of total use on each mask,  per the CDC's crisis-level shortage provisions for health care workers)—then perhaps these may be a feasible option for you, as well. Surgical masks that are not N95 or equivalent have not been approved by the CDC for reuse, due to their fitment and lower level of filtration.
 There are, of course, advantages to both medical and fabric masks depending on your needs. Naturally, the long-term reusability of cloth masks is their biggest strength. To ensure long-term safety, those masks should have some key features.
   When shopping for a good cloth mask, there are a few dos and don'ts to keep in mind, put forth by the WHO and CDC. Here's the summary.
  Look for masks with at least three layers as outlined below—the more the better. A hydrophobic middle layer of synthetic non-woven material such as polypropylene or another cotton layer which may enhance filtration or retain droplets.
 Ensure a good fit with a tight seal. Form-fitting wires in the nose bridge are especially helpful.
 Make sure it fits snugly but comfortably against the sides of the face and completely covers the nose and mouth
   Select materials that can be laundered and machine dried without damage or change to shape.
  Don't use fabrics coated in wax or other similar compounds on any layer of the mask as these can block the mask's pores for air flow, creating more air passthrough around the sides of the mask.
 Don't get masks made of stretchable fabrics as these increase pore size as they stretch.
 Sticking to these guidelines will help to ensure efficacy, reusability, and safety for yourself and others.
  Putting on, or donning, a clean mask is a relatively simple process. First, wash your hands for at least 20 seconds, then, holding the ties or ear loops, put the mask on your face, and ensure a good seal around the nose bridge, cheeks, chin, and sides of your face.
 While you're wearing the mask  do not touch the front of it, as there can be viral particles on the surface. By now you've likely heard not to touch your face (or nose, mouth, and eyes) to avoid potential infection via fomite transmission of the virus, caused by touching a surface that may be contaminated with it and introducing it to your body. While wearing your mask, the front of it especially should be treated as a potential fomite (source of transmission), so ensure proper fitment when putting it on initially to avoid having to touch it while wearing.
     This continues when you're taking off the mask, or "doffing" it, as well. Grasp only by the ear loops or ties, undo them, and remove the mask downward off your face, taking care to avoid contact with the front of the mask. Immediately place your mask in the wash after wearing; it's fine to put with other high-temperature washable clothing (more on this in the next section). If you're unable to launder the mask immediately, make sure to fold it inward so the inner part (where your face was touching) is facing out, and place it in a single disposable bag until it can be washed. Wash your hands for at least 20 seconds directly after doffing the mask.
 You should not be donning and doffing your mask multiple times throughout one wear. Masks should be washed or sanitized after every use. For this reason, it's helpful to have multiple masks to suit your wearing habits so you're never wearing a contaminated mask. Any masks exposed to outside liquids or other damage should be taken off and discarded as soon as it's safe to do so. If the layers of fabric look noticeably worn out or you notice any holes forming, discard the mask.
  Secure the mask on your face and ensure a snug, breathable fit with the best seal possible.
 Do not touch the front of the mask while wearing it or take it on and off multiple times throughout wearing.
 Take the mask off by the ties or ear loops and put it directly in the wash.
  Wash your hands for at least 20 seconds after taking off and laundering/storing your mask.
   Generally speaking, you should follow the washing or sanitizing instructions provided by the manufacturer of your mask. Do not put masks in microwaves, ovens, or attempt to use UV light to sanitize your mask. These are neither safe nor effective ways to kill all the germs on your mask. There are currently no consumer products on the market—UVC-emitting, heat-radiating, or otherwise—that can achieve sterilization of any type of mask.
 If you don't have laundering instructions for your mask, here are some general guidelines you can follow to wash your fabric mask safely and effectively.
  Masks composed of non-woven polypropylene spunbond fabric combined with cotton can tolerate high temperatures and can therefore typically be steamed or boiled.
  If hot water isn't available, wash with soap or detergent and room-temperature water, followed by either boiling the mask for one minute (if made of the proper materials) or soaking it in 0.1 percent chlorine for one minute, then thoroughly rinsing with room-temperature water to avoid any toxic residual of chlorine.
  Use bleach containing 5.25 percent–8.25 percent sodium hypochlorite. Don't use bleach outside of this range or if it's not specified.
 Check the label to see if the bleach is intended for disinfection. Some bleach products, like those made for color-safe use, don't meet the needs for disinfection.
    Prepare a bleach solution by mixing: 5 tablespoons (1/3rd cup) of 5.25 percent–8.25 percent bleach per gallon of room-temperature water
 OR 4 teaspoons of 5.25 percent–8.25 percent bleach per quart of room-temperature water
  Pour the solution down the drain and rinse the mask thoroughly with cool or room-temperature water.
   After washes, make sure to inspect the mask for any signs of damage or developing holes. Like anything made of fabric, over time, it will start to degrade. Staying vigilant here will let you know when it's time to get a new mask, especially if the manufacturer doesn't state an estimated lifetime.
  If you need to use KN95 or N95 masks, you can reuse them for up to five total uses, not to exceed eight hours collectively  as per CDC guidelines designed for health care professionals in pandemic circumstances.  Studies show that, after five uses, the integrity of the mask (its fitment in particular) has degraded beyond a safe point for use. Same goes for anything above eight hours of total use. To ensure integrity, users should  perform a seal check with each reuse.
 If you're following these procedures, then a rotation policy that allows for at least a week between each mask's use, while storing them in individual breathable paper bags in the interim, is highly advisable. This helps to limit the potential for fomite transmission from a contaminated mask while extending the use of your N95 or equivalent.
 Medical masks aren't rated for duration of use, as they're manufactured to be used once and disposed of once taken off. But the CDC indicates that following these guidelines, as well as those outlined above for  taking off PPE safely, can allow users to safely utilize the same mask on multiple occasions before disposing.
 Searching up methods for sanitizing disposable masks will bring some results, including  FDA-approved (via emergency use authorizations) and CDC/NIOSH (National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health)-endorsed methods for health care facilities to do so, specifically in the face of crisis-level respirator shortages.
 Crucially, only certain health care facilities have the proper tools to effectively use these  minimally studied strategies for sanitizing disposable medical masks. Not only do these provisions require specific, lab-grade tools and fastidious application, but they were drawn up for use in the event of worst-case-scenario shortages, where health care workers must use such methods to provide themselves some level of protection against known infected individuals. Bottom line: don't try to mimic this at home (or in public), because you can't.
 As we previously stated, there are currently no consumer products on the market—UVC-emitting, heat-radiating or otherwise—that can achieve sterilization of any type of mask. This gives washable cloth masks a huge advantage for the public.
   The best mask to limit COVID-19 transmission is the N95 respirator. When we say "best all-around," this means the best mask for the largest amount of people—healthy, low-risk individuals, in particular.
  (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
What makes  Kenneth Cole's Premium Cotton Face Mask our top all-around recommendation is the rare characteristic of not only meeting but exceeding best practices from the CDC and WHO. With six layers, comprised of an inner cotton layer, four non-woven filtration layers, and a moisture-controlled antibacterial outer layer, these masks check all the boxes—some of them multiple times. The antibacterial finish, as you'll find on any mask touting this feature, isn't certified to kill the SARS-CoV-2 virus at this point, but it is EPA registered and effective for at least 30 washes at 140°F.
 HeiQ—the chemical manufacturing company specialized in treating fabrics and textiles that makes the treatment— says the coating will kill 99.9 percent of viruses and bacteria in 30 minutes. This should help to avoid odors and potentially add another level of defense against pathogens, though the coating has not yet been proven to kill SARS-CoV-2 specifically.
 The masks are also machine-washable and have a nose clip to form-fit along your nose bridge and cheeks. This is advisable for any mask wearer but is particularly helpful for glasses wearers as this better fit works to prevent fogging up your spectacles.
 Kenneth Cole's Premium is not the  only worthwhile mask, but as far as cloth masks go, it is an exemplary representation of all the CDC and WHO's requirements. Due to its fit and adjustability (which facilitates minimal air escape), we'd also recommend this as the best mask for glasses wearers, though any on this list should do well for the same reasons.
    (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
Adding a replaceable filter to a cloth mask can be a better long-term solution for maintaining a high filtration integrity versus relying on the integrity of your mask's filter throughout multiple washes. As any breathable material begins to wear, filtration inevitably becomes affected by this. While you should still remain vigilant for signs of wear on masks with replaceable filters, the ability to swap in brand-new filters with each use means you won't have to question the integrity of your primary filtration layer. We have two selections here—one with a disposable filter and another with a washable one, though the former meets a higher filtration standard. The  Face Mask Kit by Outdoor Research uses filters made of non-woven polypropylene, which is the same material used in medical masks. They meet the ASTM F2100-2019 standard for filtration efficiency, which means they're rated to filter out greater than 95 percent of bacteria sized 0.1μm or bigger. SARS-CoV-2 viral particles can range in size from 0.5μm to less than 0.1μm. PM2.5 filters, which commonly sold online for those sensitive to air pollution, won't be very effective—they only filter out particles up to 2.5μm.
    The ASTM F2100-2019 standard also tests for differential pressure (the amount of air escaping or passing through the filter), flammability, and resistance to synthetic blood penetration. Outdoor Research's filter meets or exceeds all but the test for blood penetration, which is made specifically to simulate a patient's artery or vein bursting onto the mask—a highly unlikely situation for members of the general public to find themselves in.
 The company says its filters can be used for around five to seven uses and are not to exceed 40 hours in a week, depending on humidity, face makeup, facial hair, and sweat. Per  CDC guidelines on reusing medical masks, if not washing the Face Mask Kit every day, implementing a rotation policy using multiple masks with at least five days in between wears could be a useful strategy to prolong their filters' life span, rather than attempting to wash the mask every day and transfer the partially used filter into the freshly laundered mask.
 The Outdoor Research masks should always be worn with the filter inserted, as there's only two layers of polyester that comprise the mask otherwise. While this material is great for breathability and water repellence—two qualities the WHO recommends in a mask—its filtration efficacy alone won't provide much protection without the filter. In combination with the disposable filter, though, the polyester's low moisture retention and therefore higher electrostatic properties  could help catch aerosols. Three filters come with the mask, and replacements can be bought in  relatively inexpensive three packs.
 The face mask without the filter is machine-washable at high temperatures, which is another great plus, and it's treated with HeiQ V-Block, which is rated to kill 99.9 percent of viruses and bacteria in 30 minutes (though, as we mentioned, this hasn't yet been confirmed to kill SARS-CoV-2). This protection starts to diminish after about 30 washes, but it's unlikely you'll experience much impact from this; you should always avoid touching the front of your mask anyway.

不出所料，iFixit拆解了苹果的三款基于M1的新Mac中的两台：MacBook Air和2端口13英寸MacBook Pro。他们发现的结果在某种程度上既令人惊讶，也不令人惊讶：笔记本电脑中几乎没有任何变化，除了包含M1芯片和直接相关的变化。
 最大的变化肯定是MacBook Air省略了风扇。 iFixit指出，鉴于英特尔MacBook Air在某些情况下会出现过热的历史，它充分说明了M1的效率，到目前为止，看来Air可以在没有风扇的情况下正常运行。还缺少：在所有这些新Mac中，我们在Mac mini评测中注意到的T2芯片已完全被M1取代。
 13英寸MacBook Pro与其前身更加相似。 T2芯片也消失了，但是笔记本电脑保留了完全相同的风扇和冷却系统，没有任何区别。对13英寸MacBook Pro的评论称，风扇的旋转速度不像以前那样频繁，但是iFixit得出的结论是，这是由于从英特尔芯片向M1的转移，而不是由于冷却系统的改进。英特尔和M1 Pro上的风扇可以互换。
 不可互换的是Air中的所有零件和Pro中的零件。 iFixit感到遗憾的是，两台机器之间的相似硅片可能提供了机会，使它们可以使用其中一个零件来固定另一个零件，从而使维修变得更加容易，但事实并非如此。
 通常，由于用户可维护性和可维修性并没有朝着更加开放的方向发展，因此M1在性能和效率上的优势得到了抵消，因为M1的统一内存架构表明Apple并没有计划在不久的将来使RAM可以升级或更换。
iFixit尚未给笔记本电脑提供可维修性评分，但是这两个注释表明该评分不会高于先前型号的评分（那些机器也没有可升级的RAM）。
 有关这些笔记本电脑内部的更多镜头和其他见解，请阅读iFixit的完整拆解文章。
note_en:
As expected, iFixit has done a  teardown of two of Apple's three new M1-based Macs: the MacBook Air and the 2-port, 13-inch MacBook Pro. What they found is somehow both surprising and not: almost nothing has changed in the laptops apart from the inclusion of the M1 chip and directly related changes.
 The biggest change is definitely the omission of a fan in the MacBook Air. iFixit notes that given the Intel MacBook Air's history of overheating in some cases, it speaks volumes about the efficiency of the M1 that so far it seems the Air gets on just fine without that fan now. Also missing: the T2 chip, which we noted in our Mac mini review has been replaced completely by the M1 in all these new Macs.
 The 13-inch MacBook Pro is even more similar to its predecessor. The T2 chip is also gone, but the laptop retains the exact same fan and cooling system, with no differences whatsoever. Reviews of the 13-inch MacBook Pro claim that the fan doesn't spin up as often as it used to, but iFixit concludes here that that's because of the shift from an Intel chip to the M1, not because of an improved cooling system. The fans on the Intel and M1 Pro are interchangeable.
 What's not interchangeable are a whole bunch of parts in the Air and parts in the Pro. iFixit laments that the similar silicon between the two machines could have presented an opportunity to make repairs easier by making it possible to use parts from one to fix the other, but that seems not to be the case.
 And in general, the performance and efficiency gains of the M1 over the prior models are counterbalanced by the fact that user-serviceability and repairability are not moving in a more open direction, because the unified memory architecture of the M1 suggests that Apple isn't planning to make RAM upgradeable or replaceable any time soon.
 iFixit hasn't given the laptops a repairability score yet, but those two notes suggest the scores wouldn't be higher than those given to previous models (those machines didn't have upgradeable RAM, either.)
 For more shots of these laptops' insides and some additional insights,  read iFixit's full teardown post.
title: M1 MacBook的iFixit拆解让我们对M1有了近距离的了解
title_en: iFixit teardown of M1 MacBooks gives us our first glimpse at the M1 up close
