小型卫星发射公司RocketLab在向轨道上发送了一系列有效载荷后，首次成功恢复了电子火箭。尽管该火箭本身不会被重复使用，但该公司希望它将从随车返回地球的传感器中获取有价值的数据。卫星发射也取得了成功，这是在今年早些时候损失七颗卫星之后的一次重要验证。
   发射是在公司位于新西兰马希亚半岛的工厂进行的，在许多方面都很顺利。倒数计时顺利进行，第二阶段将有效载荷送入轨道，而脚踢飞行器将卫星分配到各个轨道。但是随着第二阶段的分离，事情变得更加复杂，工程师立即开始计算第一阶段返回地球的可能位置，或更准确地说，是返回海洋的位置。
 RocketLab将该项目命名为“ Return to Sender”，这是该公司试图将平台轻轻降低并使其恢复的首次发射。最初，可重复使用的车辆并不是RocketLab计划的一部分；正如其首席执行官在公司发射网络广播中所说的那样，电子火箭比SpaceX的Falcon小得多，并且“在小型运载工具上没有推进剂余量”来处理全动力着陆。但是，对火箭在最初几次发射期间的性能以及传感器对硬件压力的读数进行的分析表明，可能有一个更有限的返回计划。
     因此，该公司提出了一个计划，该计划将使火箭在进入大气层之前，先用一些机动发动机重新定向，然后再由小型伞形降落伞减速。一旦进入大气层，它将部署一个全尺寸的降落伞，使其降速到足以被直升机抓住的程度。返回发件人并未测试整个过程，但这是RocketLab首次尝试部署降落伞以促进平稳降落和恢复的第一个过程。这将使公司能够获得电子在重入过程中所经历的应力和温度的详细读数。
 当任务的实时流在飞溅发生之前就被切断了，该公司发布了一份声明，表明火箭已经被成功地回收，并在其Twitter提要上发布了照片证据。
在这一切发生的同时，任务的有效载荷正朝着其最后五个轨道飞行。一个是一点测试硬件，另外两个是小型海上交通监控卫星，另一个是24颗小型通信卫星的集合。最后的有效载荷来自奥克兰大学，是新西兰第一个进入太空的学生项目。
 但是最后一点有效载荷将不会保留在轨道上。 RocketLab称其为“质量模拟器”，但它是3D打印的钛花园侏儒，名为Gnome Chompski，以“半条命”游戏系列中的一款为模型。在游戏中，目标是将地精潜入火箭并将其发送到太空。据说，在发射过程中，它被发送来测试3D打印硬件的性能。但实际上，这是Valve的Gabe Newell捐钱给慈善机构的借口-向在第一天观看直播的任何人一美元。到目前为止，Gnome Chompski的推出为奥克兰星河儿童医院的儿科重症监护室筹集了80,000多美元。
note_en:
The small satellite launch company RocketLab made its first successful recovery of its Electron rocket after it had sent a collection of payloads toward orbit. While this rocket itself isn't going to be reused, the company expects that it will get valuable data from sensors that returned to Earth with the vehicle. The satellite launch was a success as well, an important validation after the  loss of seven satellites earlier this year.
   The launch took place from the company's facility on New Zealand's Mahia Peninsula and in many respects was uneventful. The countdown went off without a hitch, the second stage took the payloads to orbit, and the kicker vehicle distributed the satellites to individual orbits. But things got a bit more complicated as the second stage separated, with engineers immediately starting to calculate the likely location where the first stage would return to earth—or, more accurately, ocean.
 The launch, which RocketLab named " Return to Sender," was the first in which the company was attempting to bring the stage down gently and recover it. Reusable vehicles weren't initially part of RocketLab's plans; as its CEO said during the company's  launch webcast, the Electron rocket is much smaller than SpaceX's Falcon, and "you don't have the propellant margins on a small launch vehicle" to handle a fully powered landing. But analysis of the rocket's performance during the first few launches, as well as sensor readings of the stresses placed on the hardware, had suggested a more limited return plan might be possible.
     So the company  came up with a plan that would see the rocket reorient with some maneuvering engines before plunging into the atmosphere, slowed by a small drogue parachute. Once deeper into the atmosphere, it would deploy a full-sized parachute, slowing it sufficiently to be snagged by a helicopter. Return to Sender didn't test the full process, but it was the first in which RocketLab would attempt to deploy the parachute to facilitate a gentle splashdown and recovery. This would allow the company to get detailed readings of the stresses and temperatures that the Electron experienced during reentry.
 While the livestream of the mission cut out before the splashdown took place, the company released a statement indicating that the rocket had been successfully recovered and posted  posted photographic evidence to its Twitter feed.
  While all that was happening, the mission's payloads were heading for their five final orbits. One is a bit of test hardware, two others are small ocean traffic monitoring satellites, and another is a collection of 24 small communication satellites. The final payload was from the University of Auckland and represents the first student project from New Zealand to reach space.
 But one final bit of payload won't be remaining in orbit. RocketLab is terming it a "mass simulator," but it's a 3D-printed titanium garden gnome named Gnome Chompski, modeled on an item from the Half-Life game series. In the game, the goal was to sneak the gnome onto a rocket and send it to space. Supposedly, it was being sent to test the performance of 3D printed hardware during the rigors of launch. But in reality it was an excuse for Valve’s Gabe Newell to donate money to charity—one dollar to anyone who watched the livestream within the first day. So far, Gnome Chompski's launch has raised over $80,000 for the Paediatric Intensive Care Unit at Starship Children’s Hospital in Auckland.
title: RocketLab的“重返发件人”项目完全符合承诺
title_en: RocketLab’s “Return to Sender” launch does exactly what was promised

不是每个人都喜欢开车，也不是每个汽车都喜欢开车。很好-还有更多理由乘公交车去做那些比较琐事的旅程。但是，我们中的某些人确实对此行为感到高兴，而一些汽车公司则对此表示满意。我也不是指无法获得的超级跑车。仍然有主流OEM制造价格实惠的汽车，吸引了发烧友。例如马自达。
 去年年初，只花了几个小时-洛杉矶下午的交通量就减少了一半-意识到新的马自达3脱颖而出。它是第一款使用马自达新型Skyactiv车辆架构的汽车，尽管互联网对后悬架设计有所抱怨，但它还是一辆在道路上感觉柔顺的汽车，其控制输入和汽车反应之间呈线性关系，在这种情况下实属罕见年龄和年龄。
 当年晚些时候驾驶CX-30跨界车证明这并不是平凡的一件事，当3和CX-30都入围2019年度世界最佳汽车奖（他们以亚军身份入围）时，这一事实进一步得到了证实。到起亚特柳赖德（Kia Telluride）。但是，汽车发烧友总能找到一些抱怨的地方，而马自达3想要的一件事就是更大的动力。因此，对于2021年的模型年，您现在可以使用涡轮增压器了。它的起价为29,900美元，称为Mazda 3 Turbo。
  显然，这里的主要新事物是马自达3 Turbo的发动机。它是2.5升四缸Skyactiv-G设计，首次在CX-5跨界车中见过，尽管它具有全新的发动机调节功能，并且空气-水中间冷却器集成到进气歧管中以改善增压响应。它还为涡轮增压发动机使用了相当高的压缩比，为10.5：1。马自达表示，这是获得比同等强劲的自然吸气V6更好的燃油效率的关键。
 在自然吸气的2.5升马自达3上，功率和扭矩得到了显着提高。这辆汽车以186 hp（139 kW）和186 lb-ft（252 Nm）的速度行驶；马自达3涡轮增压器可将功率提高到250hp（186kW）和320lb-ft（433Nm）。 （如果您无法使用93辛烷值的燃油，而必须使用87辛烷值，则发动机仅产生227hp和310lb-ft。）3 Turbo仅适用于全轮驱动，并且仅适用于六速自动变速器。传播。
尽管功率增加了34％，扭矩增加了72％，加上发动机比自然吸气版重一点，但燃油效率的影响却很小。具体来说，这三个类别中的每一个都增加了1mpg（加起来为27mpg，城市为23mpg，公路为32mpg（合计8.7l / 100km，城市为10.2l / 100km，公路为7.4l / 100km）。
   悬架进行了一些微调，主要是为了应对Turbo动力总成的增加重量。因此，减震器更硬，转向臂更坚固，前弹簧刚度增加了15％。后差速器也是新的，可以应付该发动机更高的扭矩。这也催生了一种新的全轮驱动控制算法，该算法考虑了车速和G力来计算每个轮胎的垂直负载，以确定向每个车轮发送多少扭矩。对于3 Turbo，马自达希望使后轮在干燥路面上做更多的工作，而不是默认在自然吸气车等高抓地力条件下使用前轮驱动。
 最后，马自达的专家对运动模式进行了一些更改。以前，此模式更改了汽车的油门图和变速箱的变速行为。现在，它变得更聪明了。一方面，变速箱知道汽车的G负荷，并且“除非极需要升档或降档”，否则它不会在弯道中间改变。现在，在运动时，变速箱在制动时也会降档，并且马自达首次让该小按钮也修改了G矢量控制算法。这会给两个前轮中的一个或两个前轮增加或减少非常小的扭矩，以使初始转向输入更具响应性，在Sport中，这一点更为明显。
  在与马自达的问答环节中，该公司传出了一些坏消息：马自达（Mazdaspeed）子品牌已经死了而且被埋了。正如我们从其最近的其他车型中发现的那样，该公司正在寻求一种更高档的感觉，尤其是在内饰方面。因此，马自达3 Turbo并不是真正像大众高尔夫GTI或现代Veloster N这样的掀背车的竞争对手。马自达可能刚刚赢得了“赛百灵12小时耐力赛”的冠军，但在赛道上却没有像深翼的座椅或阿尔坎塔拉包裹的方向盘。实际上，仅凭一眼就很难分辨出马自达3 Turbo的低功率兄弟姐妹，因为它们的视觉暗示很微妙。排气管的直径稍大一些，后视镜的周围是光滑的黑色，空气坝，扰流板和18英寸的车轮也是如此。
 但是，这并不意味着开车并不好。它在道路上保持相同的平衡性和流动性，并渴望转弯，并且像以往一样在整个半径范围内都可以预测。但是您确实会注意到后桥有助于将您从慢速弯道中移出，涡轮增压器提供的额外动力是令人欢迎的。自动变速箱确实使情况有所降低。即使在Sport中，变化也从未像您所希望的那样快。但是，我们很难说手动变速箱并不适合该引擎。
您可以将Mazda 3 Turbo作为轿车或掀背车使用。我们测试了一辆轿车，但我更喜欢掀背车，该掀背车的后方视野不佳，但外观如此出色弥补了这一不足。轿车的价格略便宜一些，为29,900美元，舱门为30,900美元。 Premium Plus套件的价格有所不同，轿车为32,450美元，舱口为33,750美元。 （主要区别是，在获得该选装件后，舱口将获得一个新的前空气坝，而轿车则没有。）
 汽车在2021年的高级驾驶员辅助系统套件中提供了一些新功能。有一个“交通拥堵辅助”模式，用于自适应巡航控制和车道保持，可以更好地处理死锁。此外还有后方交叉路口制动，它的C形宽大柱子会在舱口中受到欢迎，然后是普通马自达3的所有ADAS。
 对我来说，所有这些加在一起就是一个引人注目的工具。但是正如我一开始就解释的那样，我被迷住了。增加一点动力，增加一些后轮驱动偏置，只会让我更加喜欢它。
note_en:
Not everyone enjoys driving, and not every car is enjoyable to drive. And that's fine—and more reason to take the bus for those journeys that are more like a chore. But some of us do take pleasure from the act, and some car companies cater to that. And I don't mean unobtainable supercars, either; there are still mainstream OEMs building affordable cars that appeal to enthusiasts. Like Mazda, for example.
 All it took was  a couple of hours at the beginning of last year—half of it in Los Angeles' afternoon traffic—to realize that the new Mazda 3 stood out from the pack. It was the first car to use Mazda's new Skyactiv vehicle architecture, and despite Internet grumblings about the rear suspension design, it was a car that felt supple on the road, with a linearity between control input and car reaction that is all too rare in this day and age.
 Driving the CX-30 crossover later that year proved it was no flash in the pan, a fact further confirmed when  both the 3 and CX-30 were finalists for the 2019 World Car of the Year award (where they came in as runners-up to the Kia Telluride). But car enthusiasts can always find something to complain about, and one thing they've wanted from the Mazda 3 is more power. And so, for model year 2021, you can now get one with a turbo. It starts at $29,900, and it's called the Mazda 3 Turbo.
  Obviously, the major new thing here is the Mazda 3 Turbo's engine. It's a 2.5L four-cylinder Skyactiv-G design, first seen in the CX-5 crossover, although here it has all-new engine tuning, and the air-to-water intercooler is integrated into the intake manifold to improve boost response. It also uses a rather high compression ratio for a turbocharged engine, at 10.5:1. Mazda says that this is key to getting better fuel efficiency than an equivalently powerful, naturally aspirated V6.
 Power and torque are increased considerably over the naturally aspirated 2.5L Mazda 3. That car gets by with 186hp (139kW) and 186lb-ft (252Nm); the Mazda 3 Turbo ups that to 250hp (186kW) and 320lb-ft (433Nm). (If you don't have access to 93 octane fuel and have to use 87 octane, the engine only produces 227hp and 310lb-ft.) The 3 Turbo is only available with all-wheel drive, and only with a six-speed automatic transmission.
 Despite this 34-percent increase in power and 72-percent increase in torque, plus an engine that's a bit heavier than the naturally aspirated version, the impact of fuel efficiency has been minimal. Specifically, a 1mpg increase in each of the three categories—a combined 27mpg, with 23mpg in the city and 32mpg on the highway (8.7l/100km combined, 10.2l/100km city, 7.4l/100km highway).
   The suspension has been tweaked a little, mostly to cope with the Turbo powertrain's added weight. So there are stiffer dampers and stronger steering arms, and the front spring rate has gone up by 15 percent. The rear differential is also new to cope with the much higher torque of this engine. That also prompted a new all-wheel-drive control algorithm, which takes into account vehicle speed and G forces to calculate the vertical load on each tire to determine how much torque to send to each wheel. For the 3 Turbo, Mazda wanted to make the rear wheels do a bit more of the work on dry pavement rather than defaulting to front-wheel drive in high grip conditions like the naturally aspirated car.
 Finally, Mazda's experts made some changes to Sport mode. Previously, this mode altered the car's throttle map and shift behavior for the transmission. Now, it has gotten a little smarter. For one thing, the transmission is aware of the car's G load, and "unless there's an extreme need for an upshift or a downshift" it won't change in the middle of a corner. The gearbox now also downshifts under braking while in Sport, and for the first time, Mazda has let that little button modify the G-Vector Control algorithm as well. This adds or subtracts very small amounts of torque to either or both front wheels to make that initial steering input more responsive, and in Sport, that's a little more pronounced.
  During a Q&A session with Mazda, the company let slip some bad news:  the Mazdaspeed subbrand is dead and buried. As we've discovered from its other recent vehicles, the company is going for a more upmarket feel, particularly with interiors. So the Mazda 3 Turbo is not really a rival to hot hatches like the  VW Golf GTI or Hyundai Veloster N. Mazda might have just won the 12 Hours of Sebring outright, but there are no nods to the race track here like deeply winged seats or Alcantara-wrapped steering wheels. It's actually hard to tell a Mazda 3 Turbo from its less-powerful siblings just by looking at them, since the visual cues are subtle. The exhaust pipe is a little bigger in diameter, and the wing mirror surrounds are glossy black, as are the air dam, spoiler, and the 18-inch wheels.
 However, that doesn't mean it isn't good to drive. It retains the same balance and sense of fluidity on the road and is as eager to turn into a corner and as predictable throughout that radius as ever. But you do notice the rear axle helping to push you out of a slow corner, and the added power that the turbocharger delivers is a welcome addition. The automatic transmission does let things down a little; even in Sport, the shifts are never quite as fast as you'd like. But a manual transmission is not in the cards for this engine, we're afraid to say.
   You can get your Mazda 3 Turbo as either a sedan or a hatch. We tested a sedan, but I prefer the hatchback, which suffers from not-great rear visibility but makes up for it by looking so good. The sedan is slightly cheaper at $29,900, with the hatch at $30,900. There's a bit more of a difference for the Premium Plus package—$32,450 for the sedan but $33,750 for the hatch. (The main difference is that the hatch gets a new front air dam when you get that option, and the sedan doesn't.)
 There are a few new features present in the car's suite of advanced driver assistance systems for 2021. There's a Traffic Jam Assist mode for the adaptive cruise control and lane keeping, which deals better with gridlock. There's also rear cross-traffic braking, which will be welcome in the hatch with its wide C pillars, and then all the ADAS from the normal Mazda 3.
 To me, all that adds up to a compelling vehicle. But as I explained right at the start, I'm smitten. Adding a bit more power and little more rear-drive bias just makes me love it even more.
title: 人们要求配备更大功率的马自达3：2021年马自达3涡轮增压评测
title_en: People asked for a Mazda 3 with more power: The 2021 Mazda 3 Turbo review

被取消的Sega VR头戴式耳机是Sega有史以来最神秘的产品之一，由于一群游戏历史保护者的成功，上周五终于以“可玩”的形式出现了。这是一个发现的ROM的故事，需要寻找其ROM的源代码，并且不仅要重建游戏，还要使现有的Genesis和Mega Drive模拟器适应现今PC耳机的虚拟现实呼叫。
 这个故事发布在视频游戏历史基金会（Video Game History Foundation）的网站上，始于游戏亚历山大（Gaming Alexandria）的迪伦·曼斯菲尔德（Dylan Mansfield）的ROM发现。有问题的游戏Nuclear Rush是Sega VR宣布的四款游戏之一，Sega VR是一款旨在插入标准Genesis和Mega Drive控制台的耳机系统。
  那个时代的游戏者可能听说过世嘉VR，因为游戏发行商的公关宣传在游戏杂志中有很多提及，在1993年的Summer CES上公开披露，甚至在ABC的Nightline上都有一小部分。但是，这个雄心勃勃的设备原定要以199美元的价格推出，但是却被悄悄取消，世嘉公司前总裁汤姆·卡林斯凯（Tom Kalinske）最终证实了原因：研究人员发现，该设备使大量测试人员感到头痛和头昏眼花。
    今天的发现在一定程度上解释了这些疾病症状可能源于何处。通过分解并了解Sega VR游戏与Genesis以及Sega VR头戴式耳机进行通信的方式，VGHF数字保护负责人Rich Whitehouse发现了头戴式耳机的严格限制：其立体图像仅刷新15Hz，而最低为72Hz。 Oculus Quest（更不用说由HTC和Valve等公司建立的90Hz标准了）。此外，Sega VR只能转换用户头部的俯仰和偏航运动，而不能滚动-这是系统的顶部，该系统已被限制为三自由度（3DOF）系统，要求用户保持坐姿。
    插件消失多年后，Whitehouse如何从Sega VR的功能中了解到很多？事实证明，曼斯菲尔德（Mansfield）每天都在搜索游戏历史勘误，其中包括向90年代的各个开发人员提出要求，要求他们将旧模型或代码藏在抽屉里。以肯尼思·赫尔利（Kenneth Hurley）为例，他作为Futurescape Productions的一部分在《核奔》中工作，他走得更高，并寄给曼斯菲尔德一张1994年8月6日的CD-ROM，奇迹般地，它并没有腐烂。
Whitehouse介入了这一点，以找出如何编译剩余的几乎完整的代码（称为“ final”，而不是“ retail final”），这需要C和汇编的混合。在Whitehouse的发现中：所写的代码仅在某些Genesis和Mega Drive硬件版本上有效，这取决于它如何处理Sprite和资产的水平和垂直滚动，而这需要进行较小的修复。此外，代码中的元数据还暗示了1994年冬季CES的Sega VR展示从未成功。
  尽管发现的CD-ROM缺少关键的Sega VR文件（怀特豪斯称其为VR.DOC和VR.TXT），但怀特豪斯仍然能够弄清楚该系统如何与16位控制台配合使用。 Sega VR IO会围绕控制台的第二个控制器端口运行，尽管Whitehouse的解释并没有阐明控制台的视频输出端口是否将被重定向到Sega VR头戴式耳机或它将如何工作。此外，Sega VR头戴式耳机将获得两张30Hz图像，然后，它们的15fps刷新将使Nuclear Rush进一步细分。
 怀特豪斯（Whitehouse）在弄清楚如何在2020年使Nuclear Rush作为VR体验工作时，与游戏的原始首席程序员Kevin McGrath进行了交谈，他证实他的团队在Sega VR上做了很多工作，而实际上没有耳机可以测试—他们发明了一项测试，该测试使游戏的视频输出在两个计算机显示器之间闪烁，以查看游戏如何处理两个耳机图像。世嘉（Sega）VR时代的另一位游戏程序员亚历克斯·史密斯（Alex Smith）证实，负责Outlaw Racing的团队甚至在项目确定之前从未动手使用过耳机原型。
    Whitehouse的其余工作围绕将OpenVR支持构建到可工作的Genesis仿真器中进行，其中包括对Sega VR面板的位置和形状进行认真的猜测，然后修复1994年代的怪癖以在现代计算机上更高效地运行（部分是为了减少Genesis时代锁定为15fps的游戏带来的潜在晕动病）。可以从VGHF文章下载并测试生成的仿真器和一对已编译的Nuclear Rush ROM。
  Ars Technica已在运行HP Reverb G2耳机的Windows 10 PC上测试了模拟器和ROM的组合，我可以确认游戏的运行与您预期的一样：这是基本的3D坦克游戏，就像Atari的经典街机游戏一样战区已经用创世纪时代的精灵和调色板进行了重建，但这全是精灵领域的诡计，而不是像90年代初期的Star Fox或Virtua Fighter那样基本的多边形东西。 （显然，世嘉VR游戏并没有像世嘉的Virtua Racing版本中那样使用世嘉SVP等额外的盒装芯片来堆满。）
最终恢复后的游戏绝不是革命性的游戏体验。尽管如此，以上列出的每个人的共同努力使游戏重获新生，其原始版本可能会让您感到恶心。值得庆幸的是，现代硬件（及其富于进取精神的用户）可以使已取消游戏复活，而不会让游戏历史爱好者折腾他们的cookie，这从整体上证明了现代游戏保存的运动。
 在视频游戏历史基金会中查看整个有趣的故事，并获得大量荒谬的技术信息。
note_en:
One of Sega's most mysterious products ever, the canceled Sega VR headset, finally emerged in a "playable" form on Friday thanks to a team of game history preservationists. It's a tale of a discovered ROM, a search for its source code, and efforts to not only rebuild the game but also adapt existing Genesis and Mega Drive emulators to translate virtual reality calls from today's PC headsets.
 The story, as posted at the  Video Game History Foundation's site, begins with a ROM discovery by Dylan Mansfield at  Gaming Alexandria. The game in question,  Nuclear Rush, was one of four games announced for Sega VR, a headset system designed to plug into standard Genesis and Mega Drive consoles.
  Gamers from that era likely heard about Sega VR, as the game publisher's PR push included plenty of mentions in gaming magazines, a public reveal at 1993's Summer CES, and even a segment on ABC's  Nightline. But the ambitious device, slated to launch at a mere $199, was quietly canceled, and  former Sega President Tom Kalinske eventually confirmed why: researchers found the device made a huge percentage of testers sick with headaches and dizziness.
    Today's discovery explains in part where those sickness symptoms likely stemmed from. By breaking down and understanding the way Sega VR games communicated with a Genesis, and therefore a Sega VR headset, VGHF digital conservation head Rich Whitehouse discovered the headset's severe limitations: a mere 15Hz refresh for its stereoscopic images, as opposed to the 72Hz minimum for Oculus Quest (let alone the 90Hz standard established by companies like HTC and Valve). Additionally, Sega VR only translated pitch and yaw movement for users' heads, not roll—and that's on top of the system already being limited as a three-degrees-of-freedom (3DOF) system, requiring that users stay seated.
    How did Whitehouse find out so much about Sega VR's functionality this many years after the add-on vanished? As it turns out, Mansfield's day-to-day search for game history errata includes requests to various '90s developers for whatever old prototypes or code they might have tucked away in a drawer. In the case of Kenneth Hurley, who worked on  Nuclear Rush as part of Futurescape Productions, he went one higher and sent Mansfield a CD-ROM dated August 6, 1994—which miraculously hadn't succumbed to bit rot.
 Whitehouse stepped in at this point to figure out how to compile the leftover nearly complete code (dubbed "final" but not "retail final"), which required a mix of C and assembly. Among Whitehouse's discoveries: the code as written only worked on certain Genesis and Mega Drive hardware revisions, based on how it handles horizontal and vertical scrolling of sprites and assets, which required a minor fix. Also, metadata in the code hinted to a Winter CES 1994 showing for Sega VR that never came to pass.
  Though the discovered CD-ROM was missing key Sega VR files (which Whitehouse says would have been named VR.DOC and VR.TXT), Whitehouse was still able to work out how the system would have worked with 16-bit consoles. Sega VR IO would have revolved around the console's second controller port—though Whitehouse's explanation doesn't clarify whether the console's video-out port would have been redirected to the Sega VR headset or how that would have worked. Additionally, the Sega VR headset would have been fed two 30Hz images, which  Nuclear Rush would have then divided further with its 15fps refresh.
 While figuring out how to make  Nuclear Rush work as a VR experience in 2020, Whitehouse talked to the game's original lead programmer, Kevin McGrath, who confirmed that his team did a lot of work on Sega VR without actually having a headset to test on—and they invented a test that had the game's video output flicker between two computer monitors to see how it might do the same with two headset images. Another Sega VR-era game programmer, Alex Smith, confirmed that the team working on Outlaw Racing never even went hands-on with a headset prototype before its project was canned.
    The rest of Whitehouse's work revolved around building OpenVR support into a working Genesis emulator, which included, among other things, doing serious guesswork about how Sega VR's panels were positioned and shaped, then fixing 1994-era quirks to run more efficiently on modern computers (in part to reduce potential motion sickness from a Genesis-era game locked at 15fps). The resulting  emulator and a pair of  compiled  Nuclear Rush ROMs are available for download and testing from the VGHF article.
  Ars Technica has tested this combination of emulator and ROM on a Windows 10 PC running an HP Reverb G2 headset, and I can confirm that the game plays about as well as you might expect: it's a rudimentary 3D tank game, as if Atari's arcade classic  Battlezone had been rebuilt with Genesis-era sprites and palettes, but it's all sprite-field trickery, not the rudimentary polygonal stuff of early '90s fare like  Star Fox or  Virtua Fighter. (Sega VR games clearly weren't being bulked up with extra on-cartridge chips like Sega's SVP, used in the Genesis version of  Virtua Racing.)
 The resulting restored game isn't a revolutionary gameplay experience by any stretch. Still, the combined efforts of everyone listed above brought life back to a game whose original version would likely have made you sick. Thankfully, modern hardware (and its enterprising users) can revive canceled games in ways that won't make game history aficionados toss their cookies, and that's a testament to the modern game-preservation movement as a whole.
 Check out the whole intriguing story, complete with a ridiculous amount of technical information, at the  Video Game History Foundation.
title: 推出了丢失的“世嘉VR”游戏，可在现代VR耳机上播放
title_en: Lost “Sega VR” game unearthed, made playable on modern VR headsets

note:
原来，一本15世纪的中世纪手稿中的页面包含隐藏的文本，这些文本仅在UV光下可见。这一发现归功于罗切斯特理工学院的一群本科生的努力，他们在班级项目中构建了自己的多光谱成像系统，并且尽管正在进行大流行，但仍设法完成了这一工作。
 在古代手稿上发现先前隐藏的文本并非前所未有。 2016年，一个国际科学家团队开发了一种“虚拟展开”在死海西海岸发现的受损严重古卷的方法，揭示了《利未记》的前几节经文。同样地，在2019年，我们报道了德国科学家使用尖端物理学技术的组合实际上``展开''了古埃及纸莎草纸，这是柏林埃及博物馆大量收藏的一部分。他们的分析表明，纸莎草纸上一个看似空白的补丁实际上包含了经过数个世纪的光照后变成“隐形墨水”的字符。
 今年早些时候，我们报告了使用多光谱成像进行的一项新分析，结果显示，英国曼彻斯特大学的四个死海古卷碎片-以前被认为是空白的-用碳墨水书写的可读文本以及部分内容字符和格线。尤其是其中一个片段显示了四行文字的残余，由大约15个字母组成。只有一个词Shabbat（安息日）是可读的，但根据分析，伦敦国王学院的琼·泰勒（Joan Taylor）认为与以西结书46：1-3中的段落有关的文字。
    因此，RIT的本科生关系很好。他们的项目于今年年初开始。这19名学生报名参加了RIT的切斯特·卡尔森影像科学中心提供的名为“创新新生体验”的课程。目的是将来自各个学科的学生召集在一起，并让他们结合自己的专业知识来解决特定问题，在这种情况下，将建立一个多光谱成像系统来分析历史文献。
 传统上，学生会在春季的一年一度的Imagine RIT：创意与创新节上介绍他们完成的项目。但是就在设备即将完成时，COVID-19大流行在3月爆发，大学也像美国其他所有教育机构一样关闭了校园。为了弥补这一损失，班上的学生分为四个较小的小组，每个小组都有不同的任务。一个小组为成像系统创建了技术手册，另一个小组编写了最终用户指南，第三小组编写了研究论文，第四小组制作了有关其项目的视频记录片。冠状病毒愿意，他们将能够参加明年的Imagine RIT。
摄影科学系学生Liz Stublen在5月份说：“在无法亲自使用系统的情况下，我们所做的真正有价值的事情是将更多有用的工作场所技能整合到我们的学习中。” “在构建系统时，我们中的一些人专注于编程和3D建模，现在我们已经能够将重点转移到整个班级，以便能够发展他们在该领域的技能。因此，即使这不是一个很好的情况，我们也已经能够扭转局面，从此时开始最大程度地受益。”
    特别是三个学生-Zoe LaLena，Lisa Enochs和Malcom Zale-花费了暑假完成了该系统。当秋天的课程恢复时，他们开始研究RIT的Cary Graphic Arts Collection中保存的15世纪中世纪手稿叶子。羊皮纸的叶子大约是十年前从历史学家和收藏家奥托·埃格（Otto Ege）那里购得的，他们定期从损坏或不完整的手稿中整理出这种叶子。当然，许多学者已经研究了该藏书。但这是第一次有人在紫外线下观察叶子。
 根据LaLena的说法，当学生对一张这样的叶子进行成像时，他们发现页面实际上是最淡淡的，有多层书写-具体来说，成像显示出“深色的法国草书”。在15世纪，羊皮纸价格昂贵，因此经常被刮擦干净并重新使用。但是即使在几个世纪后，如果人们以正确的光谱看页面，仍会保留一种讲故事的化学特征。下一步是使用他们的成像系统来分析Ege馆藏的其他29个已知书页，而这些书页也可能被淡化。
 Cary制图艺术策展人史蒂芬·加尔布雷思说：“学生们在这里至少收集了我们手稿中至少两张手稿的重要信息，从某种意义上说，他们发现了我们不知道的两本书。”采集。 “现在我们必须弄清楚这些文字是什么，这就是文化机构中光谱成像的力量。为了充分了解我们自己的收藏，我们需要了解我们收藏的深度，影像科学有助于向我们揭示所有这些信息。”
note_en:
A page from a 15th-century medieval manuscript turns out to  contain hidden text that is only visible under UV light. The discovery is due to the efforts of a team of undergraduate students at Rochester Institute of Technology, who built their own multispectral imaging system as part of a class project and managed to complete it despite the ongoing pandemic.
 It's not unprecedented to uncover previously hidden texts on ancient manuscripts. In 2016, an international team of scientists  developed a method for "virtually unrolling" a badly damaged ancient scroll found on the western shore of the Dead Sea, revealing the first few verses from the Book of Leviticus. Similarly, in 2019,  we reported that German scientists used a combination of  cutting-edge physics techniques to  virtually "unfold" an ancient Egyptian papyrus, part of an extensive collection housed in the Berlin Egyptian Museum. Their analysis revealed that a seemingly blank patch on the papyrus actually contained characters written in what had become "invisible ink" after centuries of exposure to light.
 And earlier this year,  we reported on a new analysis using multispectral imaging showing that four Dead Sea Scroll fragments housed at the University of Manchester in the UK—previously presumed to be blank—had readable text written in carbon-based ink, along with parts of characters and ruled lines. One fragment in particular showed the remnants of four lines of text, consisting of about 15 letters. Only one word,  Shabbat (Sabbath), was readable, but based on the analysis, Joan Taylor of King's College London thought the text related to the passages in  Ezekiel 46:1-3.
    So the RIT undergrads are in good company. Their project began earlier this year. The 19 students enrolled in a course known as the Innovative Freshman Experience, offered by RIT's Chester F. Carlson Center for Imaging Science. The goal was to bring students from various disciplines together and have them combine their expertise to solve a particular problem—in this case, building a multispectral imaging system to analyze historical documents.
 Traditionally, the students would have presented their completed project at the annual Imagine RIT: Creativity and Innovation Festival in the spring. But just as the device was nearing completion, the COVID-19 pandemic hit in March, and the university closed its campus, just like every other educational institution in the US. To compensate, the students in the class divided into four smaller groups, each with a different task. One group created a technical manual for the imaging system, another produced an end-user guide, a third group wrote a research paper, and a fourth produced a video documentary about their project. Coronavirus willing, they'll be able to present at next year's Imagine RIT.
    “Something really valuable that we’ve done in the absence of being able to work on the system in person is integrate more useful workplace skills into our learning,” Liz Stublen, a photographic sciences student,  said in May. “When we were building the system, a few of us were focused on programming and 3D modeling, and now we’ve been able to shift the focus for the whole class to be able to develop their skills in that area. So even though this is not a great situation, we’ve been able to turn things to maximize the benefit from this time.”
    Three students in particular—Zoe LaLena, Lisa Enochs, and Malcom Zale—spent their summer completing the system. When classes resumed in the fall they started examining the 15th-century medieval manuscript leaves housed in RIT's Cary Graphic Arts Collection. The parchment leaves had been acquired about ten years ago from historian and collector Otto Ege, who regularly compiled such leaves from damaged or incomplete manuscripts. The collection has, of course, been studied by many scholars. But this was the first time anyone had looked at the leaves under UV light.
 When the students imaged one such leaf, they found that the page was actually a palimpsest, with multiple layers of writing—specifically, the imaging revealed a "dark French cursive," according to LaLena. During the 15th century, parchment was pricey, so it was often scraped clean and re-used. But even centuries later, a telltale chemical signature remains if one looks at the page in the right spectrum of light. The next step is to use their imaging system to analyze the other 29 known pages from Ege's collection, which could also turn out to be palimpsests.
 “The students have supplied incredibly important information about at least two of our manuscript leaves here in the collection and in a sense have discovered two texts that we didn’t know were in the collection,”  said Steven Galbraith, curator of the Cary Graphic Arts Collection. “Now we have to figure out what those texts are, and that’s the power of spectral imaging in cultural institutions. To fully understand our own collections, we need to know the depth of our collections, and imaging science helps reveal all of that to us.”
title: 大学本科生通过紫外线成像在中世纪手稿上找到隐藏的文字
title_en: College undergrads find hidden text on medieval manuscript via UV imaging

note:
昨天，Apple发布了iOS 14.2.1，以修复自11月5日iOS 14.2推出以来用户在新iPhone上遇到的错误。与许多其他iOS版本不同，该版本并未随附公司所有其他操作系统的更新。
 此更新修复了一个错误，该错误特别导致了iPhone 12 mini上的锁定屏幕无响应，并且此更新解决了阻止MMS消息进入的问题。此外，14.2.1修复了已连接的助听器的声音质量问题。
  专为iPhone设计的助听器在收听iPhone音频时可能会出现声音质量问题
 先前的更新iOS 14.2较大。它添加了100多个新表情符号，将Shazam并入了控制中心，并引入了新的音频和AirPlay功能。该更新还附带了watchOS，tvOS等的更新。
 通常，数字中带有小数点后两位的更新是次要的错误修复程序，只有小数点后一位的更新是小功能更新，而只有整数（例如iOS 14）的则是年度主要版本。
iOS 14.2.1应该现在可用于受支持设备（任何带有iPhone 12名称的设备）上的所有用户。
note_en:
Yesterday, Apple released iOS 14.2.1 to fix bugs users have encountered on new iPhones since iOS 14.2 launched on November 5. Unlike many other iOS releases, this release was not accompanied by updates to all of the company's other operating systems.
 The update fixes a bug that caused an unresponsive lock screen specifically on the iPhone 12 mini, and it addressed an issue that prevented MMS messages from coming in. Further, 14.2.1 fixes a problem with sound quality on connected hearing devices.
  Made for iPhone hearing devices could have sound quality issues when listening to audio from iPhone
 The previous update,  iOS 14.2, was a somewhat larger one. It added more than 100 new emoji, incorporated Shazam in the control center, and introduced new audio and AirPlay features, among other things. That update was accompanied by updates to watchOS, tvOS, and others, as well.
 Typically, updates with two decimal points in the number are minor bug fix updates, those with just one decimal point are small feature updates, and those with just a whole number (like iOS 14) are annual major releases.
 iOS 14.2.1 should be available to all users on supported devices (any carrying the iPhone 12 name) right now.
title: Apple推出了iOS 14.2.1，主要修复了一些错误
title_en: Apple pushes out iOS 14.2.1, and it’s mostly bug fixes
